beverly-hills.Charlton Heston Former Home: The home at 2859 Coldwater Canyon Dr, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.
beverly-hills.Charo Cugat's Home: The home at 1801 Lexington Rd, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.
beverly-hills.Cher Former Home: The home at 2727 Benedict Canyon Dr, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.
beverly-hills.Chrissy Teigen Former Home: The home at 9152 Janice Place, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.
beverly-hills.Christina Aguilera Former Home: The home at 513 Doheny Rd, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.
beverly-hills.Christina Aguilera's Home: The home at 14143 Beresford Rd, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.
beverly-hills.Danny DeVito & Rhea Perlman Former Home: The home at 1028 Ridgedale Dr, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.
beverly-hills.Falcon Lair (Rudolph Valentino House): The legendary 1920s estate of silent film star Rudolph Valentino and Natacha Rambova, a symbol of Hollywood Golden Age.
beverly-hills.Jack Colkers 76 Station (HCM #1251):
The Context: Frequently referred to as "Colker's," this is perhaps the most famous gas station in the world due to its location directly within the prime commercial district.

Key Features: It is a well-known local fixture for those navigating the Triangle. It provides full-service options—a rarity in modern times—and serves as a necessary, high-visibility utility stop for the luxury vehicles frequenting the nearby boutiques of Rodeo Drive.
beverly-hills.North Arnaz Drive Historic District: A National Register of Historic Places district comprising custom garden apartments and single-family homes built between 1928 and 1952. Designed by master architect Paul R. Williams and others, the district is recognized for its cohesive garden-city planning principles and refined Modernist detailing — a rare surviving collection of pre- and post-war residential architecture in one of Southern California's most intensely developed cities.
beverly-hills.Restaurant Row (La Cienega Blvd): La Cienega Boulevard between Wilshire and San Vicente boulevards on the eastern edge of Beverly Hills grew into one of Los Angeles's finest dining corridors after World War II. At its peak, Restaurant Row hosted some of the most celebrated tables in the city, including Lawry's The Prime Rib (100 N La Cienega, est. 1938, still operating) and the original Brown Derby (9537 Wilshire Blvd, 1931–1982), alongside a rich mix of high-end steakhouses and international cuisine that defined LA fine dining for decades.
beverly-hills.RuPaul's Former Residence: RuPaul's Former Residence in The Bird Streets
beverly-hills.The Flats: The original, level residential section of Beverly Hills, north of Santa Monica Boulevard. Launched October 21, 1906 under the marketing slogan "Between the City and the Sea," The Flats was Beverly Hills's founding residential vision — larger lots, wide tree-lined streets, and a mix of Spanish Colonial, Traditional, and Ranch-style homes favored by the old Hollywood elite. Today, modern revival estates and architect-driven celebrity compounds continue the neighborhood's century-long tradition of understated, land-rich luxury.
beverly-hills.The Golden Triangle: The central business district of Beverly Hills, bounded by Santa Monica Boulevard, Wilshire Boulevard, and Canon Drive. The Golden Triangle emerged as the city's commercial core in the 1920s and has remained one of the world's preeminent luxury retail destinations ever since. It encompasses the global flagship boutiques of Rodeo Drive, the celebrated "Little Restaurant Row" on North Canon Drive, and the dense concentration of galleries, jewelers, and couturiers that have made Beverly Hills synonymous with high-end commerce.
beverly-hills.The Hillcrest Drive Modernists: A high-density stretch of single-story masterworks by architects like Paul R. Williams and Wallace Neff.
beverly-hills.The Historic Brighton Building:
Architectural Style: Spanish Colonial Revival

The History: A designated historic landmark dating back to the city's early development, this building sits at a prestigious intersection just steps from Rodeo Drive.

Key Architectural Features: The building is celebrated for its brick facade, elegant arched windows, and lush ivy-covered walls. It is currently home to Bacari Beverly Hills, utilizing the building's large, exclusive courtyard to create one of the most sought-after outdoor dining spaces in the city.
beverly-hills.The Kronish House: One of Richard Neutra's largest and most significant residential works, saved from demolition in 2011.
beverly-hills.The Samuel Goldwyn Estate (owned by Tayl: A historic landmark built in 1934 for movie mogul Samuel Goldwyn.
beverly-hills.The Sheats-Goldstein Residence: Sheats-Goldstein Residence. Designed by John Lautner, this is one of the most famous mid-century modern homes in the world. It is iconic for its poured-in-place concrete and "cave-like" atmosphere and has been featured in films like The Big Lebowski and Charlie's Angels: Full Throttle.
beverly-hills.The Sol Wurtzel Estate: Designed in 1932 by Wallace Neff, the pioneer of the 'California style,' this grand Mediterranean villa was built for powerful Fox Film Corporation producer Sol M. Wurtzel. The estate is celebrated for its distinctive semicircular design, which seamlessly follows the contours of the land to overlook the Bel-Air Country Club. It remains one of Neff's most spectacular residential achievements.
beverly-hills.The Tate Murder Site (10050 Cielo Dr): Perhaps the most infamous address in American true crime, this was the site of the 1969 Manson Family murders. While the original house was demolished in 1994 and replaced with a new mansion (and a changed address to deter 'dark tourists'), the winding, narrow road still carries a heavy, somber legacy.
beverly-hills.The William H. Levit House: A masterclass in California Mid-Century Modernism, this 1961 Richard Neutra-designed home features his signature walls of glass, terrazzo floors, and a seamless integration with the surrounding natural landscape. The home frames spectacular views stretching from downtown Los Angeles to the Pacific Ocean. Meticulously preserved and thoughtfully updated over the years, it recently changed hands in late 2025.
beverly-hills.The Witch's House (Spadena House): Originally built in 1921 in Culver City as the offices and dressing rooms for a silent film studio, this deeply eccentric Storybook-style home was later moved to Beverly Hills. With its intentionally dilapidated, steeply pitched roof, rickety picket fence, and moat, it looks like it was plucked straight out of a Brothers Grimm fairy tale.
beverly-hills.Trousdale Estates: Annexed to Beverly Hills in 1954 by developer Paul Trousdale, who transformed the former Doheny Ranch foothills into 532 custom lots. Trousdale attracted the most celebrated architects of the era — Wallace Neff, Paul R. Williams, Lloyd Wright — to design a hillside enclave of Mid-Century Modern masterpieces with sweeping views of the Los Angeles basin. Strict view-protection covenants and architectural review standards have preserved its character, keeping Trousdale Estates one of the most coveted and recognizable pockets of modernist residential architecture in California.
beverly-hills.Zoe Saldana Former Home: The home at 9551 Lime Orchard Rd, Beverly Hills, CA 90210.

beverly-hills-gateway.The Beverly Hills Hotel: The "Pink Palace," featuring additions by the legendary Paul R. Williams, who designed the iconic script logo and the Fountain Coffee Room. (The Beverly Hills Hotel)

beverly-park.North Beverly Park: Often cited as the most exclusive gated community in Los Angeles. Hidden in the hills off Mulholland Drive, it is a heavily fortified enclave of massive custom estates, famous for its intense privacy and its concentration of billionaire and A-list celebrity residents.

beverlywood.Charlie's Kosher Delicatessen:
The History: A long-standing pillar of the Pico-Robertson Jewish community, this deli has been serving traditional kosher fare for decades. It represents the deep cultural heritage of one of LA's most prominent Jewish enclaves.

Culinary Significance: Known for its traditional prepared foods and butcher services, it is a quintessential neighborhood spot where the atmosphere is as authentic as the matzo ball soup and brisket.

bouquet-canyon._neighborhood: Bouquet Canyon, once called Hangman’s or Dead Man’s Canyon, is one of many streams-carved canyons feeding the Santa Clara River. Its main drainage, Bouquet Creek, is dammed by Bouquet Reservoir on the Los Angeles Aqueduct. The canyon’s name derives from the French-speaking cowherd (vacher) who founded Bouquet Ranch after leaving a Spanish ship (buque).

boyle-heights.\"Pared\u00f3n Blanco\": Historically known as Paredón Blanco ('White Bluff') under Spanish and Mexican rule, this area served as the main camp for Californio forces during the Mexican-American War. In 1845, it became 'Pueblito' when displaced Tongva people were relocated here. Though razed in 1847 under U.S. Rule—leaving only archaeological remains—the site's deep history set the stage for Andrew Boyle's later purchase of 22 acres overlooking the river.
boyle-heights.1488 Pleasant Ave: A historic residence in Boyle Heights with architecture characteristic of the early 20th-century neighborhood.
boyle-heights.19th Century L.A. Chinese Cemetery (HCM: A sacred historical site and the final resting place for many of Los Angeles early Chinese residents from the late 1800s.
boyle-heights.2nd Street Residential Historic District: 2nd Street Residential Historic District (E. 2nd St & Boyle Ave) includes intact turn-of-the-century houses (25 properties) built 1883–1923 in Queen Anne and Colonial styles. Platted along a former First Street streetcar spur, it retains original massing, setbacks, and period streetlights.
boyle-heights.4th & Lorena St. Bridge (HCM #265): A significant concrete arch bridge that is a key component of the city early 20th-century infrastructure expansion.
boyle-heights.Al & Bea's Mexican Food: Operating out of a historic, walk-up taco shack since 1966, Al & Bea's perfectly captures the fast, utilitarian, and incredibly satisfying nature of Southern California’s classic roadside Mexican food stands. It is an essential piece of old-school Los Angeles. The undisputed champion here is the Bean and Cheese Burrito, widely considered one of the best in the city, relying on a deeply savory, luscious refried bean recipe, perfectly melted cheese, and their signature zesty salsa.
boyle-heights.Alcance Victoria (Victory Outreach): Located in a grand former movie theatre, this site represents the intersection of religious life and historic preservation.
boyle-heights.Boyle Avenue Residential Historic Distri: Boyle Avenue Residential Historic District (E. 2nd St between Boyle Ave & I-5) contains 25 Late Victorian houses built 1883–1923 in Queen Anne, Mission Revival, and vernacular styles. Designated locally for its intact streetcar-suburb character, it reflects early transit-oriented development.
boyle-heights.Boyle Heights City Hall Community Room: A primary civic meeting space housed in the historic Boyle Heights City Hall building on the vibrant 1st Street corridor.
boyle-heights.Breed Street Shul: A 1915 Byzantine-Revival synagogue that stands as a silent witness to the neighborhood's Jewish history. Its weathered facade and boarded-up windows provide a haunting visual of "Old L.A." and the demographic shifts of the 20th century.
boyle-heights.Brittania St Neighborhood Mural: Local neighborhood mural on Brittania St in Boyle Heights.
boyle-heights.Brooklyn Heights (Historic): Brooklyn Heights rose on Prospect Park’s slopes north of First St. as a late-19th C. subdivision distinct from Boyle Heights proper. Both areas fell within L.A.’s Ninth Ward (1888–1909) and were later unified under Workman’s estate.
boyle-heights.Brown Beret Headquarters: Former headquarters of the Brown Berets, a pro-Chicano organization that emerged during the Chicano Movement in the late 1960s.
boyle-heights.Canters Deli Former Site: Former site of Canters Deli on Cesar Chavez Ave.
boyle-heights.Casa 0101 (Former Site of Self-Help Grap: This corridor was once the landing spot for grassroots radicalism. The buildings here, with their faded signage and narrow alleys, evoke the atmosphere of a city transitioning from a mid-century manufacturing hub to a cultural stronghold.
boyle-heights.Centro de Comunicacion Comunitaria: A radical community bookstore and communication center that serves as a vital hub for local activism and independent literature.
boyle-heights.El Mercado de Los \u00c1ngeles: El Mercado de Los Ángeles (“El Mercadito,” 3425 E 1st St) opened 1968 on the former Boyle Heights Lumber Co. site as a multi-ethnic marketplace. Spanish-Eclectic façades enclose a red-tiled arcade around a central courtyard. Cantera kiosk (1991) and 17 benches (2001) by Jalisco artisans mark its HCM-style significance.
boyle-heights.El Tepeyac Cafe: Open since 1955, this modest, family-run institution is a cornerstone of Eastside dining. Founded by Manuel Rojas—whose larger-than-life personality was as much of a draw as the food—the cafe has fed generations of Angelenos and remains a beloved, unpretentious staple for homestyle Mexican fare. The restaurant is legendary for its massive, fork-and-knife burritos, specifically the 'Manuel's Special', weighing in at over five pounds and generously smothered in a rich, savory chile verde sauce.
boyle-heights.Estrada Courts: Estrada Courts (3232 Estrada St) opened 1942–43 as wartime low-rental housing under USHA. 30 buildings provided 214 units reserved for defense workers before becoming public housing in 1947. In the 1970s, 80+ Chicano murals transformed its facades. It’s now a cultural landmark under HACLA management.
boyle-heights.Evergreen Cemetery: Evergreen Cemetery (204 N Evergreen Ave) was established 1877 on Boyle Heights land as L.A.’s first private cemetery and potter’s field. It never barred burials by race, creating Armenian, Japanese (Issei), African American, Mexican, and Chinese sections. Its Chinese shrine is City HCM 718; the Chinese Historical Society restored it in 1992.
boyle-heights.Flor del Rio (Birrieria): A local culinary landmark highly recommended by the late Jonathan Gold, specializing in traditional Birria from Nochistlan, Zacatecas.
boyle-heights.Hollenbeck Park: Hollenbeck Park (St. Louis & Fourth St) opened 1892 on Workman and Elizabeth Hollenbeck’s donated land, replacing White Bluffs arroyo with a dammed lake, boathouse, landscaped lawns, and bridges. Spanish Revival structures and a tile-mosaic bandstand remain; I-10 runs along its western edge since the 1950s.
boyle-heights.Known as the Victorian House: Known as the Victorian House, this rare brick structure is designated as Los Angeles Historic-Cultural Monument #102.
boyle-heights.La Casa del Mariachi: Local establishment known for serving the traditional Mariachi community.
boyle-heights.La Casa del Musico: Historic local music store serving the Mariachi community on Boyle Ave.
boyle-heights.Malabar Branch Library (HCM #304): A historic public library that has served as a cultural and educational anchor for the Boyle Heights community since 1927.
boyle-heights.Mariachi Plaza: Mariachi Plaza (E 1st St & Boyle Ave) formed organically from the 1930s as mariachis gathered for hire near the Cummings Hotel. City–State of Jalisco partnership added the plaza (1992) and cantera stone kiosk (1998). A Metro L Line station beneath opened 2009. It’s Boyle Heights’s cultural gateway for mariachi music.
boyle-heights.Nagatoshi Produce: While lacking flashy Hollywood glamour, this area represents the essential, working history of the city's wholesale food distribution network. Operating as a wholesale distributor tucked into the industrial edges of the city, Nagatoshi Produce focuses specifically on Japanese produce and agricultural products, highlighting the vital intersection of agriculture, logistics, and the diverse local restaurant industry.
boyle-heights.Old Los Angeles County General Hospital: One of the most recognizable filming locations in Los Angeles, this monumental Art Deco complex is embedded in the visual memory of American popular culture. Its exterior served for decades as the establishing shot in the opening credits of the soap opera General Hospital, making it instantly familiar to generations of television viewers. Its imposing architecture has granted it remarkable versatility on screen — it has doubled as a high-security prison, a futuristic laboratory, a government complex, and a wartime hospital in productions including Network and Halloween II. Now repurposed as a USC Health Sciences campus, the historic core of the building has been preserved.
boyle-heights.Otomisan Restaurant: The oldest continually operating Japanese restaurant in Los Angeles, and the last remaining from Boyle Heights historic Japanese-American community.
boyle-heights.Pared\u00f3n Blanco (Historic): Historically called Paredón Blanco (“White Bluff”) under Spanish and Mexican rule, Boyle Heights became Pueblito in 1845 when displaced Tongva from Yaanga were relocated to springs near today’s Beverly Blvd and Soto houses. The site was razed in 1847 under U.S. rule; no built-heritage survives beyond archaeological remains. Andrew Boyle bought 22 acres overlooking the river after the Mexican–American War, naming his vineyard Paredon Blanc. His son-in-law William H. Workman subdivided the hills in 1875 as “Boyle Heights,” promoting it as a streetcar suburb. Early roads followed Native zanjas, later replaced by sewer and drainage pipes.
boyle-heights.Pico Gardens: Pico Gardens was L.A.’s ninth USHA project, built 1942 on 14 acres with 37 two-story buildings (260 units) for defense workers, later public housing. It was demolished 1997–99 for gang-free, suburban-style townhomes under a HOPE VI-like plan.
boyle-heights.Pueblo del Sol: Pueblo del Sol (formerly Aliso Village) occupies 34 acres on the riverbank. Originally built 1941–43 as Aliso Village (685 units), it was demolished under HOPE VI; Pueblo del Sol’s first two phases (377 rentals) opened 2003 as mixed-income, garden-city–influenced housing with community center, park, and adjacent Mendez Learning Center.
boyle-heights.Ramona Gardens: Ramona Gardens (2830 Lancaster Av) was L.A.’s first post-slum-clearance public housing, opened 1941 with 102 buildings and 610 units on 32 acres. Built by the Herbert M. Baruch Corp. with USHA funding, it offered modern conveniences at $11–18/month. Today it’s known for its 1970s murals, community activism, and complex social challenges.
boyle-heights.Ramona Gardens Housing Development: Public housing development associated with the Big Hazard gang; subject of sociological studies on gang culture in LA.
boyle-heights.Ramona Gardens Murals: Vibrant public murals within the Ramona Gardens housing project.
boyle-heights.Robert Vargas Square: Robert Vargas Square (1st St & State St) was dedicated December 2022 to honor Boyle Heights native Robert Vargas, the self-taught muralist celebrated for “Our Lady of DLTA” and downtown’s 14-story “Angelus” mural. The intersection naming recognizes Vargas’s global reputation and local roots.
boyle-heights.Roosevelt High School: Historic high school in Boyle Heights, known for its role in the 1968 East L.A. Walkouts.
boyle-heights.Russian Flats (Historic): Russian Flats refers to early 20th-century blocks north of Los Angeles River where Russian Molokan immigrants (Spiritual Christian sect) clustered. First recorded in 1904, families from the Kars region established communal farms, built churches, and maintained a separate cemetery—one of the country’s largest Molokan settlements.
boyle-heights.Sears, Roebuck & Company Mail Order Buil: A monumental 1927 Art Deco distribution center. Its massive 226-foot tower is a landmark of the Eastside skyline. In a "Noir" context, it represents the industrial might and the sheer scale of early 20th-century Los Angeles commerce.
boyle-heights.The Boyle Hotel-Cummings Block: The Boyle Hotel-Cummings Block, a Queen Anne-style Victorian landmark overlooking Mariachi Plaza.
boyle-heights.The Paramount / Brooklyn Ave Pizza: Historic Paramount ballroom and current site of Brooklyn Ave Pizza Co.

hcm-region-boyle-heights-east.Boyle Hotel (HCM #93): The Boyle Hotel-Cummings Block, a Queen Anne-style Victorian landmark overlooking Mariachi Plaza.
hcm-region-boyle-heights-east.Hollenbeck Park (HCM #94): Established in 1892, this Victorian-era park was the city first major lake-centric recreational space on the Eastside.

brentwood.13233 Riviera Ranch RD, LOS ANGELES, CA: An expansive estate in Brentwood designed by Cliff May, the pioneer of the California Ranch house.
brentwood.Betty White Residence (Historic Site - D: The long-time home of the beloved actress; the structure was demolished in 2022.
brentwood.Crestwood Hills:
Architects: A. Quincy Jones and Whitney R. Smith

Architectural Style: Mid-Century Modern

The History: Originally founded as the "Mutual Housing Association" in the late 1940s by a group of musicians and writers looking to create a cooperative, diverse community. It is the only successful large-scale cooperative housing experiment in California.

Key Architectural Features: The neighborhood is a treasure trove of modernist architecture, characterized by homes with post-and-beam construction, expansive glass walls, and designs that integrate into the rugged hillside topography.
brentwood.Gwyneth Paltrow's Former Estate: Hidden behind massive hedges and old-growth trees in Mandeville Canyon, this single-story Traditional/Ranch estate was built in 1950 and extensively remodeled in 2009. It was purchased in 2012 by Gwyneth Paltrow and Chris Martin, who tapped interior design firm Windsor Smith to elevate the home into a light-filled, wellness-focused oasis. Paltrow sold the estate in early 2025 for $22 million.
brentwood.Jennifer Garner Residence: The actress's estate located in the affluent Mandeville Canyon area.
brentwood.Joan Crawford Residence (Historic Site): The former home of the classic Hollywood star during the height of her career.
brentwood.Kamala Harris Residence: The Brentwood home of the Vice President of the United States.
brentwood.Larry David\u2019s house in Curb Your En: Larry David’s house in Curb Your Enthusiasm
brentwood.Nicole Brown Simpson Residence (Historic: The Bundy Drive townhouse associated with the events of 1994.
brentwood.O.J. Simpson Residence (Historic Site -: The former Rockingham estate; the original structure was demolished in 1998.
brentwood.Schnabel House by Frank Gehry: A world-renowned architectural landmark designed by Frank Gehry in 1989.
brentwood.Shirley Temple Residence (Historic Site): The childhood home of the iconic child star.
brentwood.The Barry Building: Once home to the beloved independent bookstore Dutton's Books, this commercial building is celebrated for its central, open-air courtyard and clean Mid-Century Modern lines. Built in 1951 and designed by Milton Caughey, it is officially Los Angeles Historic-Cultural Monument #887. Vacant since 2007, it has become the center of a decades-long battle between historic preservationists and developers, facing imminent threats of demolition.
brentwood.The Getty Center: The Getty Center in Los Angeles is a renowned, free-admission museum and cultural complex perched in the Santa Monica Mountains, featuring striking white travertine architecture designed by Richard Meier. Opened in 1997, it houses European paintings, drawings, sculpture, illuminated manuscripts, and decorative arts, alongside modern photography.

broadway._neighborhood: A major commercial and residential corridor in the south.
broadway.Alliance Judy Ivie Burton Tech: A high-performing South Los Angeles charter high school with a focus on technology and college preparation.
broadway.Samuel Gompers Middle School: The mission of Samuel Gompers Middle School is to provide a safe, healthy, and nurturing environment where students achieve academic excellence.

burbank.1001 Riverside Drive Burbank, CA 91506: The site of Pickwick Gardens, a historic recreation center with a 1950s bowling alley and ice rink.
burbank.1016 North Victory Place Burbank, CA 915: RC Provisions - Langer's Meat, and Tommy's Chili Wholesaler.
burbank.124 EOlive Ave, Burbank, Los Angeles, Ca:
The History: This corner (Olive and San Fernando Blvd) is historically significant as the site of Burbank's first brick building, constructed in 1888. In the city's early years, this intersection was the heart of the town, housing the local newspaper The Times and the Burbank Villa hotel.

Modern Era: Today, the site sits in the modern Burbank Village district, a testament to the city's evolution from a small agricultural town to a major commercial and media center.
burbank.1509 North San Fernando Boulevard, Burba: The Business: A quintessential example of the specialized service businesses that have anchored Burbank for decades. Woody's has been a local staple for patio and screen door solutions, reflecting the mid-century suburban residential boom that defined much of the city's northern landscape.
The Character: Formerly the site of the Buchanan Arms (a 1977 British pub), this space was transformed into an immersive, shipwreck-themed tiki bar. It features lush decor, "airlock" entrances, and pirate ephemera, representing Burbank’s love for high-concept, themed hospitality.
burbank.2018 WWest Burbank Boulevard Burbank: Members-only warehouse selling a huge variety of items including bulk groceries, electronics & more.
burbank.220 North Victory Boulevard, Burbank, CA: The Burbank Town Center serves as a major commercial landmark for the city's downtown district.
burbank.2300 WAddress Riverside Drive, Burbank: Houses Burbank Water and Power, featuring a historic 1940s Art Deco power plant.
burbank.3000 WOlive Avenue, Burbank, CA 91505: Part of the historic NBC Burbank Studios, former filming site of The Tonight Show.
burbank.3413 West Olive Avenue Burbank, CA 91505: Dimples was "America's First Karaoke Bar," the first karaoke bar to open outside of Japan. It closed its doors in January 2015.
The History: The oldest continuously operating restaurant in Burbank. This legendary chili parlor is famous for its Texas-style chili (often served over spaghetti) and its distinctive U-shaped counter.

Pop Culture Legacy: Because of its perfectly preserved mid-century interior and mountain lake mural, it has appeared in films like Once Upon a Time in Hollywood and Twin Peaks.
burbank.3520 WBurbank Blvd Burbank, CA 91505-22: Picnic-table setup where specialty pizzas are topped with ingredients like locally made sausage.
burbank.4101 Warner Boulevard, Burbank, CA 91505: The primary address and main entrance for Warner Bros. Studios, one of the most storied film lots in the world.
burbank.440 NGlenoaks Blvd, Burbank, Los Angeles: The Context: This address is part of the sprawling Burbank Town Center complex, a 1.2 million-square-foot retail hub that opened in 1991. It serves as the primary shopping anchor for the downtown area, symbolizing the city's late-20th-century urban revitalization.
burbank.4420 W. Magnolia Burbank Drive-Thru Dair: Home to the Burbank Drive-Thru Dairy, a rare example of mid-century drive-through grocery architecture.
burbank.500 S Buena Vista St: Specifically the Team Disney - The Michael Eisner Building, designed by postmodernist master Michael Graves. The building features the Seven Dwarfs as 19-foot-tall caryatids "holding up" the pediment.
burbank.619 SGlenwood Pl Burbank 91506: Kanye West's critically acclaimed album "My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy" was partially recorded at a studio at this location.
burbank.900 East Angeleno Avenue: At One Point Stored A Backyard Pirate Ship.
burbank.916 SSan Fernando Blvd Burbank, CA 91502: The site of a massive IKEA retail facility, a major regional landmark.
burbank.916 WOlive Ave, Burbank, CA 91506: The home of Corner Cottage, widely acclaimed for its legendary breakfast burritos.
burbank.ARC TV & VCR: In Burbank which, aside from being a television store, is also Burbank’s oldest art gallery. -
burbank.Bob's Big Boy (Burbank): The oldest remaining Bob's Big Boy (1949), a National Modern Landmark designed by Wayne McAllister. It is a quintessential example of Googie architecture, featuring massive glass walls and a cantilevered roof.
burbank.Bob\u2019s Big Boy (Est. 1949): A masterpiece of "Googie" architecture. It is the oldest remaining Bob’s Big Boy in the country and is a designated California Point of Historical Interest.
burbank.Bomp Records: Oldest independent label in the US.
burbank.Burbank Moose Lodge #652: The History: Established in the 1950s, the members famously built their own lodge building themselves. It serves as a vital community fraternal organization, hosting bingo nights, dinners, and events that maintain a classic, old-school Burbank social fabric.
burbank.Cartoon Network Studios: Step into the whimsical powerhouse where modern legends like Adventure Time and The Powerpuff Girls were brought to life in a building that looks like it was pulled straight from a storyboard. This iconic creative hub served as the colorful heartbeat of millennial animation, where every floor was a canvas for the most imaginative minds in the industry.
burbank.Coffee Commissary: Located in the heart of the Burbank media district, this is the definitive "industry" coffee shop. Surrounded by Warner Bros., Disney, and various production houses, its industrial-chic interior is almost always filled with people in meetings or working on scripts. It represents the modern, high-energy commercial culture of Burbank’s studio world.
burbank.Hollywood Burbank Airport: Hollywood Burbank Airport (BUR) opened in 1930 as United Airport, renamed Union Air Terminal in 1934, Lockheed Air Terminal in 1940 after Lockheed’s takeover, Hollywood–Burbank Airport in 1967 to capitalize on its proximity to studios, and Bob Hope Airport (legal name since 2003). During WWII, the Lockheed plant and airfield were camouflaged under “Operation Camouflage” to hide aircraft production facilities from potential enemy air attack, combining military engineering with Hollywood set designers.
burbank.Jim Henson\u2019s Creature Shop:
The Legacy: Founded in 1979 by the creator of the Muppets, the Los Angeles branch of this shop is where the world’s most advanced animatronics and creature suits are built.

The Context: While the New York shop handles hand puppets (Sesame Street), the Burbank facility is the hub for realistic film effects, continuing the legacy of projects like The Dark Crystal and Dinosaurs.
burbank.Lakeside Car Wash: The History: A beloved neighborhood landmark located near the Lakeside Golf Club. This full-service car wash is a quintessential piece of the upscale Toluca Lake lifestyle, known for its high-end clientele and meticulously maintained retro-modern signage.
burbank.Larry\u2019s Chili Dog:
The History: Operating since 1953, Larry’s is a "hidden in plain sight" walk-up window screened from the street by a row of hedges. It feels like a permanent secret, serving classic chili dogs and fries to locals for over 70 years.

The Vibe: The interior walls are covered in signed celebrity photos, including Steve Carell, adding a touch of Hollywood grit to this roadside staple.
burbank.Netflix Animation Studios: A sleek, state-of-the-art fortress of digital storytelling, this studio is the high-tech engine room behind global sensations like Klaus and Guillermo del Toro's Pinocchio. It represents the cutting edge of the new "Golden Age" of animation, housing a massive fleet of artists who are rewriting the rules of the medium for a worldwide audience.
burbank.Nickelodeon Animation Studio:
The History: Opened in 1998, this playful campus was designed with direct input from artists to feel like a "creative factory." It is the birthplace of global icons like SpongeBob SquarePants and Avatar: The Last Airbender.

Key Features: The facility famously features a fountain that shoots green slime-colored water and a time capsule buried on-site (relocated from their former Florida studio).
burbank.Nickelodeon Animation Studios: Originating in 1992 as “Games Animation” to produce Rugrats, Doug, and The Ren & Stimpy Show, the studio relocated from Studio City to a purpose-built Burbank facility in 1998. Now occupying over 100,000 sq ft of renovated and new LEED-Gold interiors, its headquarters on Olive Ave feature “working galleries,” character “billboards,” and screening rooms. Nickelodeon Animation has since created SpongeBob SquarePants, Avatar: The Last Airbender, and dozens of other series that define Nicktoons.\n\nThis vibrant, slime-colored playground is the mad laboratory behind SpongeBob SquarePants and The Loud House. It remains a riot of pure creative energy, featuring a sprawling campus where the most eccentric minds in the industry bring the next generation of cartoons to life.
burbank.Otto's: Is A Hungarian Deli in Residential Burbank! 2 -
burbank.Pinocchio Restaurant (Monte Carlo Deli): The History: A multi-generational family tradition that has been in Burbank since 1971. Part authentic Italian deli (Monte Carlo) and part cafeteria-style restaurant (Pinocchio), it is famous for its oven-baked sandwiches and old-world atmosphere.
burbank.Safari Inn: A beloved retro motel that has retained its classic 1955 Googie aesthetic, complete with an iconic neon sign. It has served as a quintessential Southern California backdrop in numerous films and shows, most notably Quentin Tarantino's True Romance.
burbank.Taco Bell with Star Photos: One of the many former child stars (and a few adults) whose photos adorn this unassuming burbank taco bell restaurant -
burbank.The Arnolds' House in \"The Wonder Years: Located on a quiet, quintessential suburban street, this private residence provided the timeless exterior shots for the beloved 1980s television series The Wonder Years, perfectly capturing the nostalgic essence of Anytown, USA.
burbank.The Burbank Studios (formerly NBC Studio: Constructed 1952–55 as “NBC Color City,” the six-story complex at 3000 W Alameda Ave was RCA’s first facility built for live color television. Architects embedded underground tunnels linking Studios 1–4, designed for multitrack audio, remote broadcasts, and live network programming. Iconic shows taped here include Hollywood Squares (1966–80), Rowan & Martin’s Laugh-In (1968–73), and Johnny Carson’s Tonight Show (1972–92). In 2014 NBC sold the lot, renaming it The Burbank Studios; the Peacock signage remains a visual landmark.
burbank.The Starlet Apartments:
Architectural Style: Mid-Century Modern

The Context: A classic two-story residential building from the late 50s. Its name and location near the major studios evoke the era when young actors and industry professionals flooded into Burbank during the golden age of television production.
burbank.The Tally Rand Diner: A beloved local diner that has maintained its original 1950s roadside charm for decades. The Tally Rand is anchored by a classic, large-scale neon sign that has served as a beacon along San Fernando Boulevard for generations of Burbank residents and passersby. Inside, comfortable booths, a welcoming counter, and an unpretentious neighborhood atmosphere preserve the traditional diner experience that has made it a local institution.
burbank.Walt Disney Animation Studios: As the ultimate cathedral of storytelling, this legendary studio is the modern-day heart of the Disney legacy where classics from Snow White to Encanto were forged. Its striking Sorcerer’s Hat building serves as a soaring beacon of imagination in the center of the Burbank Media District.
burbank.Walt Disney Elementary School: The iconic global headquarters of the Walt Disney Company. Unlike other major lots, Disney does not offer standard public tours, making this campus an exclusive, closed-door hub of animation and film history. The lot is instantly recognizable from the 135-foot-tall Sorcerer's Apprentice water tower and the Team Disney building, whose roof is held up by massive statues of the Seven Dwarfs.
burbank.Walt Disney Studios: Financed by Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1937), Disney’s 51-acre Streamline Moderne lot at 500 S Buena Vista opened February 23 1940. Kem Weber’s design placed ink-and-paint labs, a three-story animation building, and Stage 1–4 around a central plaza. The studio later expanded with Stages 16 and 22 (1937), the Mill Building, and backlot sets for Zorro and Disneyland TV. It served as Walt Disney’s corporate HQ and remains home to Walt Disney Animation, Marvel Studios, and ABC Television.
burbank.Warner Bros. Animation: The historical engine room of Looney Tunes wit and DC superhero action, this studio carries the torch of Chuck Jones and Tex Avery into the 21st century. It is the ultimate factory of kinetic comedy and epic storytelling, where the world's most iconic characters continue to evolve.
burbank.Warner Bros. Studio: First National Pictures built the 62-acre lot in 1926; Warner Bros. acquired control in 1928 after the success of The Jazz Singer. Stage 16 (1937) became one of the world’s largest soundstages, complete with a 2 million-gallon water tank. The lot fostered pre-code gangster classics (Little Caesar, 1931), Looney Tunes, and film noirs (Casablanca, 1942), and later TV hits (Friends, Two and a Half Men). Today, the 110-acre facility—replete with 36 stages and backlot streets—anchors Warner Bros. film & television production worldwide.

burbank-culver-mcm.Astro Motel: A quintessential space-age motel with sharp angular rooflines and a futuristic aesthetic characteristic of mid-century roadside culture.

cadillac.Corning: (also known as La Cienega Heights) The neighborhood originally called Cadillac–Corning adopted the name La Cienega Heights in 2003. Bounded by Cadillac Avenue, La Cienega Boulevard, Sawyer Street, and Robertson Boulevard, it encompasses roughly 10–18 square blocks of predominately single-family homes. Its demographic shifted from an early substantial Jewish population to primarily African American and Latino residents.

calabasas._neighborhood: Calabasas occupies 14 miles along the southwest corner of the San Fernando Valley and the Santa Monica Mountains. Originally part of Rancho San Vicente and Rancho El Escorpión, the area hosted Chumash villages, Spanish expeditions in 1769–1776, and Miguel Leonis’s “King of Calabasas” era in the 1870s. Incorporated in 1991 after decades as a rural enclave, Calabasas now balances historic preservation with gated residential estates and open-space protections.
Living history museum focusing on the old West, depicting California ranch life in the 1880s.
calabasas.5068 N. Old Scandia Lane, Calabasas, 913: 5068 N Old Scandia Lane Calabasas, CA - Los Angeles Pet Memorial Park - photo by sRe.
calabasas.Bellagio at Calabasas:
The Context: Located within the prestigious, double-gated community of The Oaks, "Bellagio" refers to a specific ultra-luxe enclave within the development.

Neighborhood Character: Known for its massive Mediterranean-style estates and extreme privacy, it has become a modern-day synonymous with celebrity residency in the Santa Monica Mountains foothills.
calabasas.Kanye West's clothing company YEEZY form: Kanye West's clothing company YEEZY former HQ
calabasas.Leonis Adobe: One of the oldest surviving structures in the Los Angeles area, the Leonis Adobe dates to circa 1844. A Monterey Colonial-style rancho, it was home to Miguel Leonis and is now a museum preserving the rancho era of California history.

canoga-park._neighborhood: Canoga Park (formerly Owensmouth): Founded as Owensmouth on March 30, 1912, the townsite was platted at the western edge of Isaac Van Nuys’s wheat ranch in anticipation of water from the Owens River Aqueduct. Annexed to Los Angeles in 1917 to secure a municipal water supply, it was renamed Canoga Park on March 1, 1931. Today it remains a largely residential San Fernando Valley neighborhood, its early Red Car and Sherman Way transit history commemorated by local preservation groups.
canoga-park.20914 Roscoe Blvd: Canoga Park, CA 91304 - DeSoto Pharmacy - still has a malt shop.
canoga-park.21004 Sherman Way Canoga Park, CA 91303-: The Madrid Theatre, a historic performing arts venue and cultural anchor for the West Valley.
canoga-park.21413 Vanowen Street, Canoga Park, CA:
The History: Opened in 1970 as a small juice bar and health food store, this location is the birthplace of "Vegenaise." It remains a counter-culture landmark in the West San Fernando Valley.

Culinary Significance: It is one of the oldest vegetarian restaurants in Los Angeles, serving as a pioneer for the plant-based movement long before it became a mainstream trend.

Key Features: It represents the vibrant "thrifting" culture of the Valley, featuring over 180 vendors of antiques, architectural salvage, and vintage commercial typography, reflecting LA's obsession with mid-century aesthetics.
canoga-park.8960 Eton Ave: Rainbo has pressed records for just about every major artist, including Led Zeppelin, Snoop Dogg, The Beatles, Coldplay, Beck, Elvis Presley, 2Pac, Madonna, Radiohead, and many more.
canoga-park.Canoga Park Community Center: A central San Fernando Valley venue used for neighborhood council meetings and diverse local community events.
canoga-park.Komal: 2026 Emerging Chef James Beard Foundation Award Nominee. Fátima Juárez’s acclaimed Canoga Park spot featuring traditional Mexican flavors with modern techniques.
canoga-park.Robbins Bros (Engagement Ring Store): A landmark of 'superlative' Valley retail architecture on Topanga Canyon Blvd.

canterbury-knolls._neighborhood: A hillside pocket within the greater South LA area.

carson._neighborhood: While Los Angeles has an official "Historic Filipinotown" (HiFi) near Echo Park, Carson is widely recognized as a major cultural hub for the Filipino-American community, sometimes colloquially referred to as a "Little Manila" of the South Bay due to its high concentration of Filipino businesses and residents. A central node for this is the area surrounding Carson Street and Main Street, where numerous Filipino eateries and markets are located.
carson.19800 Main Street, Carson, CA 90745: Home to the Porsche Experience Center Los Angeles, a 53-acre destination featuring a driver development track and the historic Restaurant 917.
carson.22600 Wilmington Avenue Carson, CA: The site of the Valero Wilmington Refinery, a massive industrial landmark that supplies a significant portion of the asphalt used in Southern California.
carson.Alameda Corridor:
The History: This location sits directly on the Alameda Corridor, a 20-mile high-capacity "rail expressway" that connects the national rail system to the ports.

Significance: It is a site of immense industrial scale, where the infrastructure of global trade—massive freight trains and shipping containers—meets the urban fabric of the South Bay.
carson.Carson Industrial Infrastructure:
The Context: This address is located within the massive industrial heart of Carson, an area defined by the logistics and petroleum industries that fuel the Ports of Los Angeles and Long Beach.

The Character: The surrounding landscape is dominated by large-scale refinery infrastructure and sprawling distribution centers, representing the gritty, essential "engine room" of the Southern California economy.
carson.Dignity Health Sports Park: A world-class multi-sport complex that serves as the home of the LA Galaxy. It is a premier venue for professional soccer, tennis, and boxing, often pulsating with the energy of global sporting events.
carson.DJ Bibingkahan: A beloved local gem dedicated to the craft of traditional Filipino comfort food, specifically the bibingka (charcoal-baked rice cake). With its warm, slightly charred crust and coconut-infused center, it provides a cozy, authentic taste of Philippine holiday traditions all year round.
carson.Dominguez Hills: Home to the California State University, Dominguez Hills (CSUDH) campus and the Dignity Health Sports Park.
carson.Grill City: An aromatic theater of traditional Filipino BBQ, where the smokey sizzle of pork skewers and the charred perfection of liempo (grilled pork belly) take center stage. It offers an authentic "turo-turo" (point-point) dining experience that brings the flavors of a Manila street market to the South Bay.
carson.Jollibee: The global phenomenon and undisputed heavyweight of Filipino fast food, where the legendary "Chickenjoy" fried chicken and signature sweet-style spaghetti offer a nostalgic, joyful taste of the Philippines. It is the ultimate community gathering spot, famous for its cheerful mascot and uniquely delicious island-inspired menu.
carson.Keystone: An industrial-heavy section of the city.
carson.Porsche Experience Center Los Angeles: A high-octane 53-acre playground for automotive enthusiasts. This facility features a specialized driver development track where visitors can push legendary sports cars to their limits, anchored by the sleek, historic-themed Restaurant 917.
carson.Red Ribbon Bakeshop: A decadent wonderland of artisanal Filipino sweets, renowned for its light-as-air Mango Supreme cake and rich, buttery ensaymadas. This bakery is a celebratory staple for the community, turning every occasion into a feast with its iconic rolls and traditional pastries.
carson.Seafood City Supermarket: Far more than a grocery store, this massive marketplace acts as the bustling "town square" for Carson’s Filipino residents. It is a sensory explosion of tropical produce, fresh seafood, and hard-to-find imports, serving as the anchor for the local Filipino cultural experience.
carson.Stevenson Park: A vibrant community green space named after Adlai Stevenson, featuring expansive ball fields and a gymnasium. It serves as a social anchor for the northern residential pockets of the city.
carson.Valero Wilmington Refinery: A towering industrial titan that defines the city's skyline. This refinery is a critical infrastructure landmark, supplying a massive portion of the asphalt and fuel used to keep Southern California moving.
carson.Victoria Park: A massive 36-acre regional park that features a popular golf course and a world-class aquatic center. It is a premier destination for South Bay recreation, blending manicured landscapes with active sports facilities.
carson.West Carson: An unincorporated area adjacent to the city of Carson, often associated with the Harbor-UCLA Medical Center corridor.

carthay.Carthay Center Elementary School Garden: School garden at Carthay Center Elementary School on West Olympic Blvd.
carthay.Olympic Castle: Known locally as the Olympic Castle for its distinctive castle-like architecture on West Olympic Blvd.

los-angeles-carthay-circle.Carthay Circle HPOZ:
The History: Developed in the 1920s and 30s by J. Harvey McCarthy, this was one of the first planned communities in Los Angeles to feature underground utilities.

Neighborhood Character: Named after the legendary (now demolished) Carthay Circle Theatre, the HPOZ preserves a stunning collection of Period Revival architecture, primarily Spanish Colonial and Monterey Colonial homes, centered around a unique radial street plan.

castaic.31557 Castaic Rd, Castaic, CA 91384: A key service location for the regional logistics industry, notably housing Benny Diesel Road Service.
castaic.Castaic Junction: A key transportation crossroads in the Santa Clarita Valley where the 5 and 126 highways meet.

central._neighborhood: An industrial and residential hybrid area.
central.27th Street Bakery Shop: Located on 27th Street in South Central, a historic family-owned business famous for its sweet potato pies.
central.Fred Roberts Recreation Center: A bustling neighborhood park and facility providing essential recreational and after-school programs in South L.A.
central.Pueblo Del Rio public housing project: Constructed in 1941 by legendary architects Paul R. Williams and Richard Neutra, this National Housing Administration project originally housed low-income factory laborers and returning veterans. Located at 5th and Alameda and locally known as 'The Pueblos' or 'Low Bottoms,' its two-story brick-and-stucco buildings cluster around open lawns, reflecting decades of complex demographic shifts, community resilience, and historical struggles in South L.A.
central.SLA Shootout Site: The location of the 1974 televised shootout between the Symbionese Liberation Army and the LAPD.

century-city._neighborhood: Century City is a 176-acre neighborhood and business district on Los Angeles’s Westside, located about ten miles west of Downtown. Developed on the former backlot of 20th Century Fox—land once owned by silent-era cowboy star Tom Mix—the district’s first office building, Gateway West, opened in 1963. Today its cluster of skyscrapers, mixed-use developments, and retail centers make it one of Southern California’s most prominent employment hubs.
century-city.Fox Studio Lot: The Fox Studio Lot occupies roughly 50 acres at the southwest edge of Century City, bounded by Santa Monica Boulevard, Avenue of the Stars, Pico Boulevard, and Olympic Boulevard. Opened in 1928 as Fox Movietone City, it was among Hollywood’s first purpose-built sound film facilities, featuring Spanish Colonial Revival offices alongside architecturally eclectic soundstages. In 1961, Fox sold 176 acres of backlot land to Alcoa—retaining and leasing back about 75 acres—to finance the creation of Century City. Today the lot retains 15 soundstages, from Egyptian-themed halls to the famed scoring stage where The Sound of Music was recorded, plus backlot streets, commissary eateries, and state-of-the-art post-production suites. It continues to operate as a fully-functioning studio facility, offering production and post-production services while preserving nearly a century of film and television heritage.
century-city.Modern Family House: The house used as the exterior of the Dunphy family home in the television show Modern Family.
century-city.Nakatomi Plaza (Fox Plaza): The iconic 34-story skyscraper used as the primary setting for the 1988 action classic Die Hard.

chatsworth.21532 Devonshire Street Chatsworth, CA 9: The Munch Box, a classic 1950s hamburger stand and LA Historic-Cultural Monument #750.
chatsworth.21635 Devonshire Street Chatsworth, CA: The Cowboy Palace Saloon, a legendary "honky-tonk" famous for live country music.
chatsworth.23350 Lake Manor Drive- Chatsworth muse: The site of ARTVANTA, a contemporary art museum and gallery specializing in unique private collections.
chatsworth.9361 Farralone Ave, Chatsworth, CA frank:
Architect: Pereira & Luckman

The History: Completed in 1951 for Chase Manhattan Bank heiress Dora Hutchinson, this 10,000-square-foot mid-century modern masterpiece sits on over 13 acres. Frank Sinatra famously rented the estate for nearly a decade throughout the 1950s, using it as a secluded playground for his "Rat Pack" and Hollywood's elite (including a legendary rumored rendezvous between JFK and Marilyn Monroe in the guest house).

Cinematic Legacy: Heavily guarded and highly private, the estate has earned a reputation as one of America's most frequently filmed private residences, serving as a backdrop for high-profile shows like Mad Men and Californication, as well as countless major motion pictures.
chatsworth.9800 Baden Avenue Chatsworth, CA: A private residential property located near the scenic rock formations of Stoney Point Park.
chatsworth.Harvester Farms (Birthplace of Mr. Ed): L.A. Historic-Cultural Monument #645; historic horse ranch and pop-culture landmark.
chatsworth.Iverson Ranch (Lone Ranger Rock): Historic movie ranch; the definitive backdrop for the Golden Age of Western cinema.
chatsworth.Lawrence Middle School: A sprawling Chatsworth educational campus that frequently serves as a local polling place and community meeting site.
chatsworth.Leading Auto Body: Served as a filming location for the movie Punch-Drunk Love.
chatsworth.Mature Olive Trees (Lassen Street): A remnant row of mature olive trees along Lassen Street in Chatsworth, planted during the early agricultural development of the western San Fernando Valley. These trees are living evidence of the region's ambitions as an olive oil and fruit-producing area before suburbanization.
chatsworth.Minnie Hill Palmer House: The last surviving homestead cottage in the San Fernando Valley (1911).
chatsworth.Nike Missile Site LA-88: Located in the rugged Santa Susana Mountains, this abandoned Cold War site features concrete launch pads and administrative ruins that once housed nuclear-capable missiles.

While the show is set in Jenkintown, Pennsylvania, this Cheviot Hills home provided the quintessential 1980s suburban look needed for the series. It is a frequent stop for TV fans looking to see the real-life "Goldberg" driveway and front porch.

chinatown.First Cemetery of Los Angeles (Site of): The site of Los Angeles' first official cemetery, established in the Spanish Colonial period. The site was later relocated and redeveloped, but remains an important marker of the city's earliest layers of settlement and mortality.
Industrial food distribution located near Naud Junction.

The plaza is the historical heart of the city, surrounded by El Pueblo de Los Angeles Historical Monument and overlooked by the Old Plaza Church. It is where California history as a city literally began.

chinatown.Nuestra Se\u00f1ora la Reina de Los Ange: Established in 1784, the Plaza Church (Nuestra Señora la Reina de Los Angeles) is one of the oldest standing buildings in Los Angeles. It has served the spiritual needs of the city through Spanish, Mexican, and American eras, and remains an active Catholic parish at the heart of El Pueblo.

compton.Jesse Robinson Olympic Park: A community park that still holds a weathered, fading set of the iconic Olympic rings from the 1984 Summer Games, serving as a somber link to LA's sporting history.

covina.Covina Bowl: Elaborate Egyptian-themed Googie bowling alley; part of a recent adaptive reuse project.
covina.Heritage House: A 1908 residence that serves as a pristine example of the 'Citrus Era' wealth; featuring built-in cabinetry and stone masonry typical of the American Craftsman movement.
covina.The Lawnmower Building (Bigbee): A 1961 commercial masterpiece with a 'folded plate' zigzag roofline and floor-to-ceiling glass, designed to catch the eye of 1960s motorists.

crenshaw.Former Kabuki Sukiyaki: This address holds a rich layer of local musical history beneath its current function as a Social Security office. Before its demolition, this corner was the home of the Kabuki Sukiyaki restaurant and lounge in the late 1960s, a vital gathering space. This site is legendary in the local Chicano music scene: in 1969, East L.A. producer Eddie Davis discovered the house band playing a Latin-Jazz and R&B cover called 'Viva Tirado'. After being signed and renamed El Chicano, their track became a massive hit, cementing this exact location as the birthplace of the classic 'Eastside Sound'.
