torrance.Alpine Village: A Bavarian-themed shopping and dining village established in 1968, known for its kitschy chalets and Old World charm.
torrance.Buffy Summers House: The exterior filming location for 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer'; a classic 1914 Craftsman home.
torrance.Del Amo Fashion Center: Del Amo Fashion Center, once the largest mall in the U.S. and the site of key scenes in Jackie Brown, Bad Santa, and Valley Girl.
torrance.Eddie Adams' Childhood Home (Boogie Nigh: This suburban house in Northwest Torrance served as the home where a young Eddie Adams (Dirk Diggler) frequently clashed with his demanding mother before running away to join the adult film industry in Paul Thomas Anderson's Boogie Nights (1997).
torrance.First Franchised Taco Bell Site (Taco Si: The site of the first ever franchised Taco Bell location, now a local favorite taco shop.
torrance.Former Site of the Driftwood Motel: Historically the site of the Driftwood Motel, a 1950s "tropical modern" landmark. In its heyday, it was a premier roadside destination known for its luxury suites and heated pool, representative of the South Bay's mid-century hospitality culture.
torrance.Historic Old Torrance Residence: Located in the historic heart of Old Torrance. This area was part of the original "Planned Modern Industrial City" designed by the Olmsted Brothers (landscape architects of Central Park) and features architecture influenced by the legendary Irving Gill.
torrance.Madrona Marsh Preserve: One of the last remaining vernal marshes in Southern California, a critical stopover for migratory birds.
torrance.Rocketship Park: Formally Los Arboles Park; famous for its vintage 1960s rocketship climbing gym and panoramic views.
torrance.Skyline Mobile Park: Home to the Skyline Mobile Park, a prominent residential community known for its central location near the scenic beaches of the South Bay and the Palos Verdes Peninsula.
torrance.Torrance High School: Opened in 1917, this historic Mediterranean Revival campus on the National Register of Historic Places served as West Beverly High in Beverly Hills, 90210 and Sunnydale High in the cult-classic horror series Buffy the Vampire Slayer.
torrance.Torrance High School (Sunnydale & West B: Torrance High School, arguably the most famous school in Hollywood history. It stood in for "West Beverly High" in 90210, "Sunnydale High" in Buffy the Vampire Slayer, and the primary setting for She's All That.
torrance.Torrance Memorial Medical Center: The location of the Torrance Memorial Medical Center, a major regional healthcare landmark that has served the South Bay community since 1925.
torrance.Zamperini House: The 1912 family home of WWII hero Louis Zamperini; a cornerstone of Historic Old Torrance.

tujunga.Bolton Hall: A 1913 stone building constructed by single-tax colony farmers, Bolton Hall served as the civic and social center of the Little Lands Colony of Tujunga. It is a significant example of early 20th-century community self-reliance and utopian settlement in the San Fernando Valley foothills.
tujunga.Bolton Hall Museum: Bolton Hall, a 1913 fieldstone community center and utopian landmark (HCM #2).
tujunga.E.T. House: The suburban home of Elliott and his family in Steven Spielberg's 1982 blockbuster.
tujunga.McGroarty Arts Center: The Arts & Crafts-era home of John Steven McGroarty, California's Poet Laureate and author of the famous 'Mission Play.' The property reflects the Arroyo Culture aesthetic that flourished in the foothills communities of the early 20th century.

universal-city.Monkey Island: A mid-century curiosity that featured a man-made island where dozens of monkeys lived and played, surrounded by a moat. It was a popular roadside stop for travelers heading through the pass between Hollywood and the San Fernando Valley.
universal-city.Psycho House and Bates Motel: World-famous examples of 'set architecture' that have become permanent historic landmarks on the Universal backlot.

university-park._neighborhood: The area surrounding the USC campus.
university-park.Alfred J. Salisbury Residence (1891): A masterpiece of the Queen Anne style, the Salisbury Residence is celebrated for its sweeping wrap-around porch and distinctive corner tower. This grand home captures the transitional spirit of the 1890s, blending refined Victorian details with a massive, commanding presence that remains one of the most photographed landmarks in the University Park neighborhood.
university-park.Automobile Club of Southern California: A 1923 Spanish Colonial Revival landmark that served as the headquarters of the Automobile Club of Southern California, one of the most powerful organizations shaping the car-centric development of Los Angeles. It represents the foundational role of the automobile in defining the city's geography and culture.
university-park.Casa de Rosas Campus: A historic campus that has served variously as a private school, an inn, and a refuge for the homeless.
university-park.Doheny Mansion: A sprawling French Chateau-style estate built in 1899 for oil tycoon Edward Doheny, located within the Chester Place gated community.
university-park.Doheny Mansion (Chester Place): The grand estate of oil magnate Edward Doheny, built in 1900 in the Chester Place private street enclave near USC. The mansion and its grounds represent the opulent peak of West Adams as L.A.'s wealthiest residential district. Now part of Mount Saint Mary's University.
university-park.Felix Chevrolet Sign: Towering over the 110 freeway near downtown Los Angeles, the massive three-sided neon sign featuring Felix the Cat has been a beloved beacon for commuters since 1958. Felix the Cat was licensed to the dealership's original owner, Winslow Felix, who was friends with the character's creator, Pat Sullivan. The sign is deeply ingrained in LA's car culture and is one of the largest and most famous neon automotive signs on the West Coast. The dealership itself has been operating since the 1920s, making it one of the oldest in the city.
university-park.Glen Lukens Home and Studio (1940) \u201:
The Vibe: Designed for the world-renowned ceramicist Glen Lukens. This is a rare industrial-residential hybrid. The studio was built specifically to accommodate Lukens' massive kilns and experimental pottery work.

Gazetteer Note: Unlike the hillside glass boxes, this is a more grounded, "working-class" modernist structure. It proves Soriano’s belief that steel could be utilized for utilitarian, creative spaces just as effectively as for luxury homes.
university-park.Historic Districts: Includes University Park HPOZ, North University Park Historic District, and St. James Park Historic District.
university-park.Holbox: 2025 Outstanding Chef James Beard Foundation Award Winner. Chef Gilberto Cetina Jr.'s Michelin-starred Yucatan seafood specialist inside the Mercado La Paloma food hall.
university-park.John C. Harrison House (1891): This striking Queen Anne Victorian stands as a premier example of 19th-century elegance, featuring the intricate woodwork and asymmetrical silhouettes that defined the era's opulent residential architecture. As a contributing property to the North University Park Historic District, it serves as a beautifully preserved anchor for a streetscape that once housed Los Angeles' social elite.
university-park.Menlo Avenue-West 29th St Historic Distr: Recognized as a Historic District with significant early 20th-century residential architecture.
university-park.Paul Revere Williams House: Historic residence on W 35th St associated with architect Paul R. Williams.
university-park.Shrine Auditorium: Massive Moorish Revival performance venue built in 1926.
university-park.St. Vincent de Paul Church: A breathtaking 1925 Spanish Colonial/Churrigueresque masterpiece designed by architect Albert C. Martin. Financed heavily by oilman Edward L. Doheny, its massive, ornate tiled dome is one of the most recognizable and striking religious landmarks in Los Angeles.
university-park.Thomas Churchill Mansion (Arbuckle/Bara): A grand historic mansion famously associated with silent film stars Roscoe 'Fatty' Arbuckle and the legendary 'vamp' Theda Bara. It reflects the era when West Adams was the premier residential district for early Hollywood royalty.
university-park.W.W. Cockins House (1894): Designed by the prominent architectural firm Bradbeer & Ferris, this 5,570-square-foot Queen Anne manor is a designated Los Angeles Historic-Cultural Monument (#519). Beyond its architectural pedigree, the house holds a place in Hollywood lore as a rumored temporary residence for Charlie Chaplin in 1913, adding a layer of cinematic history to its Victorian grandeur.
university-park.Widney Hall (USC): The oldest university building in Southern California, built in 1880 as the first home of the University of Southern California. The modest Eastlake Victorian structure is a stark contrast to the modern campus that surrounds it, serving as a founding monument of higher education in Los Angeles.

valley-glen.The Great Wall of Los Angeles: Running for half a mile inside the Tujunga Wash flood control channel, this massive mural depicts a timeline of California's history with an emphasis on minority and indigenous populations.
valley-glen.Valley Plaza Library: A community-focused library branch serving the Valley Glen and North Hollywood neighborhoods.
valley-glen.William Mellenthin Birdhouse Apts: Apartment complex featuring 'birdhouse' cupolas typical of local builder William Mellenthin.

valley-village.12345 Magnolia Boulevard in Valley Villa: This casual hangout features sports on many TVs, karaoke & a variety of cocktails & Texas BBQ.
valley-village.Faith Presbyterian Church: A neighborhood spiritual center that offers its social hall for community meetings and local outreach programs.
valley-village.Marilyn Monroe's First 'Star' Home: The modest, white-stucco apartment complex where Norma Jeane lived when she was first discovered; a primary pop-culture landmark of her transition to Marilyn.
valley-village.The 'Neutral' Filming Streets: The gold standard for 'Anywhere, USA' location scouting; a grid of meticulously kept traditional homes used by major studios to represent anonymous American suburbs.
valley-village.The Isabel Residence (Bewitched): This East Coast Traditional-style home is a notable filming location, serving as the residence of Isabel (Nicole Kidman) in the 2005 film adaptation of Bewitched. It is representative of the high-quality residential architecture built by studio workers who founded the neighborhood in the 1930s.
valley-village.Valley Village 'Adobe' Houses: A cluster of 1930s-era Spanish-style cottages featuring thick walls and rounded archways, representing the neighborhood's early pre-WWII development phase.

van-nuys.14310 Oxnard Street, Van Nuys, CA 91401: Rock Corporation former location of this club.
van-nuys.14426 Friar Street: Odd used book store Bargain Books.
van-nuys.14535 Keswick St, Van Nuys, CA 91405: Valentin Machine Shop‎ - photo by lavocado.
van-nuys.14924 Vanowen Street Los Angeles, CA 914: Homes to people in Van Nuys - poverty and blight.
van-nuys.15237 Sherman Way: Site of the Van Nuys Muffler Man, a classic fiberglass roadside "giant."
van-nuys.16320 Raymer St. Van Nuys, CA 91406: When you drive through the streets of industrial buildings surrounding it, you first encounter a vintage Army ambulance, painted olive-drab, with a red cross, at the entrance of the tree-shaded parking lot.
van-nuys.16829 Saticoy Studio: One of the 'Studios in Surprising Places' mentioned in local lore.
van-nuys.17322 saticoy van --nuys atomic cycles: Cool bicycle store offering tune-ups, repairs & custom-built two-wheelers, plus painting & welding.
van-nuys.6192 Tyrone Avenue, Van Nuys, CA 91401: Scene from 'Me and You and Everyone We Know' script.
van-nuys.6842 Valjean Avenue, Van Nuys: John Dykstra - Special Effects Warehouse used for 'Star Wars'.
van-nuys.7021 Hayenhurst Studio: One of the 'Studios in Surprising Places' mentioned in local lore.
van-nuys.7301 Van Nuys Blvd, Van Nuys, Los Angele: 7301 Van Nuys Blvd, Van Nuys, Los Angeles, California 91405 - La Carreta Salvadorian Food‎ - Valley's favorite Salvadorian.
van-nuys.7551 Woodley Avenue: Airport Smoke Center - airportsmokecenter.com‎ - Weed Store.
van-nuys.Anheuser-Busch Brewery: This massive facility was used as a primary filming location for the 1980s sci-fi miniseries V.
van-nuys.Anheuser-Busch Brewery & Former Busch Ga: The Anheuser-Busch Brewery, a massive 95-acre industrial landmark opened in 1954. The facility is a major regional employer and once featured "Busch Gardens," a popular bird sanctuary and theme park that operated on the grounds until the late 1970s.
van-nuys.Atomic Cycles: A local community hub for custom bicycle culture and vintage repairs.
van-nuys.Bargain Books: An "odd" and well-loved used bookstore known for its eclectic and dense collection.
van-nuys.Beeps Diner: Beeps Diner, a cherished local institution that has been serving classic American diner fare from this location since 1956.
van-nuys.Bill\u2019s Burgers (Bill's Drive-In): A true "unvarnished" legend. For over 50 years, Bill Elwell served burgers from this tiny, cash-only yellow shack. It is a portal to mid-century Van Nuys, featuring a simple menu, a small counter, and a grill that has seasoned thousands of patties. It remains one of the most storied and least-changed burger stands in the entire Valley.
van-nuys.Burman Industries: Home to Burman Industries, a specialized supplier that has provided materials for Hollywood special effects, makeup, and prop-making for decades.
van-nuys.Cameron Woods (Orion Avenue): Located in the "Cameron Woods" section, Orion Avenue is frequently cited as the most filmed residential street in the San Fernando Valley and the world, appearing in hundreds of commercials and films.
van-nuys.Dunder Mifflin / The Office: This industrial building served as the exterior and primary filming location for the NBC series The Office. It was also the site for Snoop Dogg’s "Drop It Like It's Hot" music video.
van-nuys.Historic Arby's Hat Sign: One of the few remaining "Giant Hat" neon signs from the chain’s mid-century era.
van-nuys.Historic Postwar Carpentry Shop: Identified by SurveyLA as a significant postwar carpentry shop that provided essential materials for the San Fernando Valley's massive construction boom in the 1940s and 50s.
van-nuys.Jet Stream Liquor: Features a Bob Hope Airport-themed neon sign. Note: Located on the border of Van Nuys and North Hollywood.
van-nuys.Me and You and Everyone We Know: A primary residential filming location for Miranda July's indie classic.
van-nuys.NORMS Restaurant (Sherman Way): A NORMS Restaurant, part of the iconic Southern California chain founded in 1949. The chain is world-famous for its Googie architecture and its status as a "neighborhood destination" for casual family dining.
van-nuys.Oscar Niemeyer Inspired Building: Designed by a student of the legendary Brazilian architect Oscar Niemeyer, featuring distinct modernist lines.
van-nuys.Police Station Filming Location (Twins): A section of this building (primarily facing Erwin Street) served as the police station in the film Twins starring Arnold Schwarzenegger and Danny DeVito.
van-nuys.Sound City Studios: Home to the legendary Sound City Studios. Opened in 1969, it is one of the most successful recording studios in music history, famous for its "signature sound" (particularly for drums). It was the site where Nirvana recorded Nevermind, and served as the creative birthplace for iconic albums by Fleetwood Mac (Rumours), Tom Petty, Neil Young, and Johnny Cash. It was the subject of the 2013 documentary Sound City directed by Dave Grohl.
van-nuys.Star Wars Special Effects (ILM): This was the site of John Dykstra’s special effects warehouse where Industrial Light & Magic (ILM) was born and the visual effects for the original Star Wars (1977) were created.
van-nuys.Tillman Water Reclamation Plant (Japanes: Famous as the location of Starfleet Academy in the Star Trek universe and the "Pleasure Garden" in Star Trek: The Next Generation.
van-nuys.Van Nuys City Hall: A major San Fernando Valley municipal hub housing city departments and public hearing rooms.
van-nuys.Van Nuys Civic Center: A significant collection of mid-century government architecture, representing the centralized growth of the Valley in the 1960s.
van-nuys.Van Nuys HPOZ:
Architectural Significance: The area acts as a timeline of early 20th-century suburban expansion. It begins with Craftsman and Colonial Revival homes, shifts to Spanish Colonial and English Revival in the 1920s, and transitions into Minimal Traditional and early California Ranch styles. Because the area was dominated by poultry farming in the 1920s, many of the lots are unusually deep to accommodate the historic chicken houses that once sat at the rear of the properties.

The History: Established in 2005, this was the very first Historic Preservation Overlay Zone (HPOZ) in the San Fernando Valley. It protects a neighborhood that features some of the earliest residential development in the Valley, with single-family homes dating back to 1911.
van-nuys.Van Nuys Iceland (Former Rainbow Rink): Historically known as the Rainbow Rink (opened in 1950), this site is famous as a venue where Ritchie Valens performed "La Bamba". In 1960, it became Van Nuys Iceland and has operated as an ice skating rink ever since.
van-nuys.Van Nuys Savings and Loan Building: The Van Nuys Savings and Loan building, an excellent 1954 example of Mid-Century Modern architecture with Googie influences. Designed by notable architect Culver Heaton, the interior is famous for containing a mural by the master artist Millard Sheets.
van-nuys.Van Nuys Woman's Club: The site of the Van Nuys Woman's Club, built in 1917. It is designated as Los Angeles Historic-Cultural Monument #201 and represents one of the oldest social organizations in the San Fernando Valley.
van-nuys.Wayne's World House: This was the location of Wayne Campbell’s (Mike Myers) house in the 1992 film Wayne's World.

venice.1077 Elkgrove Ave, Venice, CA 90291: Designed by Ralph Vaughn - was one of the first African-American architects in Los Angeles and in the United States to work on anything as large or as significant as a housing complex with hundreds of units.
The Museum of Jurassic Technology, an internationally acclaimed and deeply eccentric artistic and educational institution.
venice.1512 pmao's kitchen acific avenue venice: This casual Chinese eatery serves up country-style dishes with a side of Mao-themed humor.
venice.220 Lincoln Blvd, Venice, CA 90291: Long-running Mexican eatery with a Bogart theme serving classic eats with a tequila cart & guitar.
venice.23 Windward Avenue Venice, CA 90291-4102: This historic address served as the apartment for the character Bobby in the 1979 disco-skating film Roller Boogie.
venice.513 Rose Ave, Venice, CA 90291 \u2014 Ve: Cereal art covers the walls of this snug cafe offering a creative breakfast & lunch menu.
venice.63 Navy St Venice, CA 90291: No-frills liquor shop with on-site ATM offering wine, beer, food & some convenience items.
venice.700 Palms Blvd Venice, CA 90291: This quirky 1940s home has been covered inside & out with colored tile & splintered-glass mosaics.
venice.842 SBroadway, Los Angeles, CA: 842 S Broadway, Los Angeles, CA‎ - Optometric Eye Institute,.
venice.Ballerina Clown Sculpture (Jonathan Boro: Iconic 30-foot Ballerina Clown sculpture by Jonathan Borofsky mounted above the CVS on Main Street, one of Venice's most recognized public artworks.
venice.Binoculars Building (Public Art 'Giant B: A Frank Gehry-designed office complex featuring a monumental 45-foot-tall binocular sculpture by Oldenburg and van Bruggen that doubles as an entrance.
venice.Coop Himmelblau House (513 Grand): An avant-garde residential design by the renowned Austrian firm, representing the deconstructivist influence on Venice architectural landscape.
venice.Dogtown (Historic):
The History: "Dogtown" refers to a historic geographic area spanning South Santa Monica and Venice. In the 1970s, this gritty, declining coastal neighborhood birthed a revolution in youth culture.

Cultural Significance: It was the stomping ground for the legendary Z-Boys (Zephyr Competition Team), who pioneered aggressive, surf-inspired skateboarding. Utilizing empty swimming pools during the California droughts, these local teens transformed skateboarding from a niche hobby into a global action-sports phenomenon.
venice.El Bordello Alexandra: This is the site of El Bordello Alexandra, one of the most visually striking apartment buildings in Los Angeles. Features a massive collection of demonic leering gargoyles, life-sized warrior-woman statues, and vibrant murals. The building was transformed by owners Tony Wells and Brittany Stevenson from a dilapidated structure into a "bizarre" landmark intended to serve as a communal creative crash pad for artists and musicians.
venice.First Baptist Church of Venice: Historic Black church in the Oakwood neighborhood of Venice.
venice.Historic Venice Beach Home (Clad in Holl: An outstanding Venice beach home, fully renovated on a rare double lot. The original Hollywood Bowl benches from 1919 clad the home exterior.
venice.Irvin Tabor Family Residences: Homes owned by Irvin Tabor, an African American who worked as Abbot Kinney's chauffeur and inherited properties in Venice.
venice.Jim Morrison Historical Site: A historical site associated with Jim Morrison of The Doors.
venice.John Baldessari\u2019s former Venice stu: This property served as the studio and gallery for the groundbreaking conceptual artist John Baldessari. It represents a vital, creative piece of the vibrant Venice contemporary art scene.
venice.Mosaic Tile House: A 1940s bungalow completely encrusted in vibrant tile and glass mosaics; a decades-long art project with an elaborate sculpture garden.
venice.Spiller House: A striking early example of Frank Gehry's corrugated metal aesthetic, consisting of two dense vertical living spaces.
venice.The Eames Original Offices (The 901 Stud: This location is historically significant as the Eames Original Offices (the 901 Studio). Served as the creative headquarters for legendary designers Charles and Ray Eames from 1943 until 1988. It was the birthplace of some of the most influential designs of the 20th century, including the Eames Lounge Chair and their groundbreaking film and exhibition work. Abbot Kinney Boulevard itself is now world-famous as a premier destination for high-end boutique shopping and produce-forward dining.
venice.Venice High School (Rydell High): The main exterior and outdoor campus location for Rydell High School in the 1978 musical Grease. Also featured in American History X.
venice.Venice Lifeguard Station: Iconic lifeguard headquarters on Ocean Front Walk.
venice.Venice Post Office: Historic post office building featuring New Deal-era murals.
venice.Westminster Elementary Auditorium: A vibrant neighborhood school auditorium located on the famous Abbott Kinney strip, often used for community forums.
venice.Winston House: Winston House. This well-known live music venue and social club is housed in the oldest building in Venice.

vermont-knolls.10th Wonder of the World Sculpture Yard: A prominent front-yard installation of colorful structures made from industrial pipes and found materials; a significant piece of South L.A. street-level lore.
vermont-knolls.Mark Ridley-Thomas Constituent Service C: A South L.A. administrative hub providing local access to county services and community resources.
vermont-knolls.St. Brigid Catholic Church: Built in 1953; a Romanesque-modern landmark featuring unique stained glass and a minimalist mid-century facade.
vermont-knolls.Vermont Knolls Historic District: A concentration of Spanish Colonial Revival homes (1928-1940) notable for elliptical street patterns that break the traditional urban grid.

vermont-square._neighborhood: Home to one of LA's oldest library branches.
vermont-square.Challengers Boys and Girls Club Track &: Park/Facility in Vermont Square / South LA
vermont-square.Vermont Square Library: A historic 1913 Carnegie library known for its classic Italian Renaissance architecture and local community service.

vermont-vista._neighborhood: The southernmost residential strip of the region.
vermont-vista.Mom's Bar-B-Q: A no-frills take-out shack serving up hearty portions of ribs, baked beans and sweet-potato pies.

vermont-slauson._neighborhood: A major intersection and surrounding residential pocket.
vermont-slauson.Slauson Super Mall:
The History: While the user noted this as Gardena, this specific address is located in the heart of South Los Angeles. Formerly a massive aerospace manufacturing plant, it was converted into one of the most iconic indoor swap meets in the United States.

Cultural Significance: The Slauson Super Mall is a cultural epicenter for the local community and the broader West Coast hip-hop scene. It is famous for its custom jewelry, urban fashion, and as a breeding ground for local creative entrepreneurs. It represents a significant era of adaptive reuse, turning a defunct industrial site into a thriving marketplace.

vernon.2Pac / Thug Life Photoshoot Location: The industrial train yards used by photographer Mike Miller for the iconic 1994 "Thug Life" photoshoot featuring Tupac Shakur.
vernon.Alameda Swap Meet: A vibrant cultural marketplace known for traditional Mexican food, apparel, and live music—a sharp contrast to the surrounding gray industrial landscape.
vernon.Evy of California (Hello Kitty): A major apparel manufacturing hub specifically known for producing licensed Hello Kitty and Sanrio merchandise.
vernon.Farmer John Murals: Massive, iconic folk-art murals depicting a pastoral "hog heaven." Painted by Les Grimes and later Arno Jordan, they wrap around the entire meatpacking plant.
vernon.Fashion 21 (Forever 21 Original HQ): The early corporate and logistics headquarters for the global fast-fashion giant back when it operated under the "Fashion 21" moniker.
vernon.General Mills Flour Mill: General Mills, Inc. - Flour Mill for huge cereal producer.
vernon.Harkham Industries (Late Moderne Landmar: A stunning example of Late Moderne industrial architecture. It features streamlined horizontal bands and sophisticated geometric detailing typical of the post-WWII era.
vernon.Historic Wig-Wag Signal: Location of one of the last operational "wig-wag" magnetic flagman railroad signals in California before its removal/theft in 2020. A pilgrimage site for industrial historians.
vernon.La Villa Basque (Now La Villa Grill): A mid-century "time warp" established in 1960. Originally a French-Basque restaurant and coffee shop, it served as the unofficial social headquarters for Vernon’s founding families.
A staple of Vernon’s heavy industrial identity, representing the city's massive metal distribution and processing sector.
vernon.Santa Fe Art Colony: A historic 1916 furniture factory converted into the city's first rent-stabilized artist live/work colony. It features classic "daylight factory" architecture with massive industrial windows.
vernon.Sterling Ruby Studio: The massive industrial workspace of world-renowned contemporary artist Sterling Ruby. The scale of the building allows for the production of his signature large-scale sculptures and textiles.
vernon.Tapat\u00edo Hot Sauce Headquarters: Tapatio Hot Sauce is the headquarters of one of the most recognizable condiment brands in the world, situated in the strictly industrial city of Vernon. The facility represents the classic manufacturing history of Vernon, which was the first exclusively industrial city in the Southwestern United States. The city of Vernon itself is a "hidden gem" for industrial architecture, featuring massive warehouses and distribution centers that define the logistics backbone of Los Angeles County.
vernon.Zankou Enterprises: The administrative and catering/delivery hub for the legendary L.A. chicken chain, strategically located to serve the city’s vast workforce.

view-park.View Park-Windsor Hills Historic Distric: One of the largest National Register-listed districts in the U.S.; a premier collection of mid-century modern and Post-and-Beam homes once known as the 'Black Beverly Hills'.

warner-center.The Trillium: Geometric twin bronze-glass towers representing the 1970s/80s 'Edge City' development of the West Valley.
warner-center.Warner Center Towers: A dense high-rise complex defining the Valley's skyline, marking the transition from ranch land to an urban center.

watts._neighborhood: Famous for the historic Watts Towers and its deep civil rights history.
watts.Council District 15 Watts Office: A localized municipal service center providing direct access to city council representation for Watts residents.
watts.Cultural Significance: Historic African-American neighborhood with deep cultural roots. Site of the 1965 Watts Riots, which sparked major social change.
watts.Hawkins House of Burgers: Renowned for serving some of the best "bargain-basement" burgers in the city.
watts.Watts City Hall / Engine No. 65: Historic former city hall for the independent city of Watts, now a fire station.
watts.Watts Happening Center: Community center active since the aftermath of the 1965 Watts Uprising.
watts.Watts Station: The historic Pacific Electric Railway station that gave the Watts neighborhood its name. Founded as a stop on the interurban rail line, the station was the nucleus around which the community of Watts developed in the early 20th century.
watts.Watts Towers: Simon Rodia's iconic folk art masterpiece.
watts.Watts Towers (Simon Rodia): Created by Italian immigrant laborer Simon Rodia over 33 years (1921-1954), the Watts Towers are one of the greatest works of outsider art in the world. The 17 interconnected structures, covered in shells, tile, and glass, rise up to 99 feet and are a testament to one person's extraordinary vision and obsessive, self-taught artistry.

west-adams._neighborhood: A massive historic district with some of the city's grandest mansions.

Local bargain shop on W Adams Blvd.

west-adams.Braids By Sabrina: Local hair braiding salon on W Adams Blvd.
west-adams.Buster Keaton & Natalie Talmadge Residen: The Italian Renaissance-style former home of silent comedy genius Buster Keaton and actress Natalie Talmadge. It is a testament to the sheer concentration of early film stars who lived in the historic West Adams district.
west-adams.Club Fais Do-Do: Historic West Adams music venue on W Adams Blvd.
west-adams.Congregational Church of Christian Fello: Historic congregational church on S Hobart Blvd.
west-adams.Constance Talmadge Residence: The impressive former home of silent film star Constance Talmadge, further cementing the footprint of early Hollywood's greatest names in the grand, wide boulevards of South Los Angeles.
west-adams.Dandelion Ranch: Community garden and urban farm on W Jefferson Blvd.
west-adams.Doheny Mansion (HCM #30): The opulent estate of oil tycoon Edward L. Doheny.
west-adams.Fisher & Sons Funeral Home (Six Feet Und: A stunning Queen Anne Victorian serving as the iconic Fisher family home and funeral parlor in the HBO series Six Feet Under.
west-adams.Golden State Mutual Life Building (Paul: 1948 by Paul R. Williams at Western Ave & Adams Blvd. A significant monument to Black corporate success in the mid-century.
west-adams.Guasti Villa / Busby Berkeley Estate: Built in 1910 by an Italian wine magnate, this spectacular Beaux-Arts villa was later owned by legendary Hollywood choreographer Busby Berkeley. Today, it serves as the Peace Awareness Labyrinth & Gardens, an unexpected spiritual oasis in the city.
west-adams.Hauerwaas Residence: Notable historic residence on W 27th St in West Adams.
west-adams.J-N-J Burger & Bar-b-que: Local burger and BBQ joint on W Adams Blvd.
west-adams.Johnny's Pastrami on Adams: Classic LA pastrami stand on W Adams Blvd.
west-adams.Kinney Heights: Developed in 1900 by Abbot Kinney, featuring custom-built Craftsman mansions.
west-adams.Kobbler King: Local cobbler and shoe repair on W Jefferson Blvd.
west-adams.London Shop Barber Shop: Long-standing local barbershop on W Adams Blvd.
west-adams.Longstreet's 'Palm Avenue':
Located near the corner of West Adams Boulevard and Flower Street, this row of 23 Mexican Fan Palms (Washingtonia robusta) constitutes some of the oldest living residents of Los Angeles. They represent the very beginning of the city's transformation into a "palm-guarded" Mediterranean oasis.

Origins: These trees were planted around 1875 by Charles Longstreet, a wealthy New Yorker (and distant relative of Confederate General James Longstreet) who moved to Los Angeles for his health. He established a 35-acre estate called L'Orangerie.

The Driveway: The palms originally lined the grand private driveway that led from Adams Boulevard to the Longstreet mansion. At the time, this area was the "hinterlands" of the city, dominated by orchards and sprawling estates.

Significance: While Los Angeles is now synonymous with palm trees, they are not native to the L.A. Basin. Longstreet's "Palm Avenue" was one of the first major ornamental plantings that helped establish the palm as a symbol of Southern California luxury and exoticism.

The Site Today: Though the mansion is long gone, the trees were preserved when the land was donated to the Orthopaedic Institute for Children (OIC) in the 1920s. Today, they stand nearly 100 feet tall, visible from the nearby 110 Freeway, serving as a biological "ghost" of the Victorian-era estates that once defined the West Adams district.

west-adams.Mariscos Maria's Restaurant: Seafood restaurant on W Adams Blvd.

west-adams.Marvin Gaye Former Residence: Former residence of soul legend Marvin Gaye in West Adams.
west-adams.McWorld (Art Venue): Art venue and gathering space on W Adams Blvd.

west-adams.Rindge-Frederick Hastings House: A significant West Adams estate reflecting the grand residential ambitions of early 20th-century Los Angeles, part of the historic corridor of landmark homes that made West Adams the premier address in the city before World War II.
west-adams.RPM International (Ray Charles Studio): The legendary recording studio and headquarters built by Ray Charles in 1962. Blending modernism with a fortress-like exterior, this hidden creative powerhouse was where the Genius of Soul recorded some of his greatest work.
west-adams.Saint Vincent de Paul Church: A 1925 Spanish Churrigueresque baroque masterpiece designed by Albert C. Martin Sr. Its ornate facade and soaring interior are considered some of the finest examples of Catholic church architecture in the United States. A spiritual anchor of the Adams Boulevard corridor.
west-adams.Second Church of Christ Scientist: A monumental Beaux-Arts temple on the Adams corridor, representative of the grand civic and religious architecture built for West Adams' wealthy turn-of-the-century residents.
west-adams.Second Church of Christ, Scientist: A monumental 1910 Beaux-Arts spiritual center that defines the grand scale of historic Figueroa Street.
west-adams.South Seas House: Distinctive Polynesian Revival residential property on W 24th St.

west-adams.The Forthmann House: A pristine 1887 Queen Anne Victorian; a survivor of the grand residential era of West Adams.
west-adams.The Giraffe House: Residential property named for its distinctive giraffe-themed exterior decor.
west-adams.The House Bunny Sorority House Filming L: The house used as the sorority house in the 2008 film The House Bunny.

west-adams.The Wilfandel Club: Founded in 1926 by prominent Black women including Della Williams (wife of architect Paul R. Williams), it is the oldest African American women's club in Los Angeles. It remains a vital community hub and a pillar of the 'Sugar Hill' legacy.
west-adams.The Witwicky House (Transformers Filming: The house used as the Witwicky family home in the 2007 film Transformers.

west-adams.William Andrews Clark Memorial Library: Built in 1926 and donated to UCLA, the Clark Library is a world-class research library housed in a spectacular Italian Renaissance Revival building. Its collection of rare books, including a major collection of Oscar Wilde manuscripts, is internationally significant. The surrounding formal garden is a masterpiece of early 20th-century landscape design.

west-adams-heights.First African Methodist Episcopal Church: A 1968 masterpiece designed by Paul R. Williams; a primary spiritual and social anchor for the African-American community.
west-adams-heights.Peace Awareness Labyrinth and Gardens (G: A 1910 Italian Renaissance villa once owned by Busby Berkeley; features a marble labyrinth and multi-tiered meditation gardens.
west-adams-heights.The Beckett Mansion: A six-story 1912 mansion that serves as a pristine example of the Beaux-Arts influence on L.A.'s early elite residential tracts.

west-athens.Mrs. Parker's House: This South LA residential stretch is famous as the primary filming location for the 1995 film Friday.
west-athens.The Friday House (Craig's House): This South LA residential stretch is famous as the primary filming location for the 1995 film Friday.

west-athens-westmont._neighborhood: Athens-Westmont and West Athens-Westmont are terms often used interchangeably by government agencies to describe this large unincorporated area of Los Angeles County. Unlike surrounding communities, it falls outside the City of Los Angeles city limits and is policed by the L.A. County Sheriff's Department rather than the LAPD. Bounded roughly by the City of Los Angeles to the north and east, Inglewood and Hawthorne to the west, and Gardena to the south, the area is anchored by Los Angeles Southwest College and the Metro C Line (Green) Vermont/Athens Station.

west-covina.Hurst Ranch Historical Center: A preserved 1906 pioneer ranch and museum that serves as a reminder of the valley's pre-suburban citrus and walnut industry.
west-covina.West Covina City Hall: A 1969 Brutalist landmark featuring an inverted-pyramid profile and open public plazas; designed to modernize the city's suburban identity.

los-angeles-west-hills.24124 Welby Way West Hills CA 91307: Brad & Stacy's House in Fast Times At Ridgemont High -Phoebe Cates.
los-angeles-west-hills.Orcutt Ranch Horticulture Center: The estate of William Warren Orcutt, a pioneering geologist and oil industry figure, featuring a 1920s Spanish Revival ranch house surrounded by extensive citrus groves and a heritage oak woodland. Now a public horticulture center.
los-angeles-west-hills.Shadow Ranch House: A late 19th-century ranch house that was once the centerpiece of a large eucalyptus plantation. It is now a community center and park, preserving a rare piece of early San Fernando Valley agricultural history.

west-hollywood.8520 Santa Monica Blvd, West Hollywood,: 8520 Santa Monica Blvd, West Hollywood, Los Angeles, California 90069 - The spinning chicken head at Koo Koo Roo in West Hollywood - photo from the flickr of the City of West Hollywood.
west-hollywood.Adaptive Reuse Corridor (Near Formosa Ca: This segment is characterized by "adaptive reuse" architecture. It is located near the historic Formosa Cafe (7156), a legendary 1939 film-industry hangout and Los Angeles Historic-Cultural Monument.
west-hollywood.Asthma Vapineze Sign: A notable piece of vintage roadside signage.
west-hollywood.Book Soup: Legendary independent bookstore on the Sunset Strip since 1975.

west-hollywood.El Mirador Apartments: An ornate 1929 Spanish Colonial Revival and Churrigueresque apartment block designed by Leland A. Bryant for early screen stars.
west-hollywood.F. Scott Fitzgerald Death Site: The apartment building where the author of The Great Gatsby spent his final days and passed away in 1940.
west-hollywood.Formosa Cafe (Est. 1925): A legendary noir-era hangout for mobsters and movie stars. The interior is made of old Red Car trolleys.
west-hollywood.Formosa Caf\u00e9: Historic Hollywood institution opened in 1925, famously housed in a decommissioned red Pacific Electric trolley car.
west-hollywood.Hauser & Wirth | WEHO: Hauser & Wirth presents a dynamic schedule of exhibitions, collaborating with renowned curators to present museum quality surveys and invests considerable resources in new scholarship and research.
west-hollywood.Historic 1920s Spanish Colonial Bungalow: Located in the Melrose District transition zone. These addresses feature the classic 1920s and 30s Spanish Colonial Revival bungalows that characterized West Hollywood before the high-rise development of the 1960s.
west-hollywood.Historic French Market Place Site: The French Market Place (Historically). While currently under redevelopment, this site was a landmark of 1970s and 80s LGBTQ+ activism and social life, serving as a "safe haven" and community meeting place during the early years of the city’s incorporation.
west-hollywood.Historic Production Studios Adjacency (T: Directly adjacent to The Lot Studios (formerly United Artists Studio and Pickford-Fairbanks Studios). This is one of Hollywood’s most historic production sites, founded by Mary Pickford and Douglas Fairbanks in 1919.
west-hollywood.Hollywood Riviera: Designed by the influential architect Edward Fickett.
west-hollywood.Howard Hughes Headquarters: Originally built in 1930, this fortress-like property was owned and inhabited by Howard Hughes and served as the command center for his vast business empire.
west-hollywood.Joey's Cafe: Joey’s Cafe, a beloved neighborhood pet-friendly staple. Known for its extensive outdoor patio and classic California breakfast menu (like the signature avocado toast), it has been a local gathering spot for decades in the heart of WeHo.
west-hollywood.Lloyd Wright Home and Studio: Built in 1927 by Lloyd Wright, this residence showcases his textile block system, featuring intricate pre-cast concrete blocks and lush landscaping.
west-hollywood.Marlene Dietrich & Marilyn Monroe (Roman: A historic apartment complex that has hosted stars including Marlene Dietrich and Marilyn Monroe.
west-hollywood.Mid-Century Luxury Apartments (Near Suns: Located just off the Sunset Strip, this address sits in a neighborhood of high-density "luxury" mid-century apartments. Clark Street is historically significant for its proximity to legendary music venues like the Whisky a Go Go and The Viper Room.
west-hollywood.Mutato Muzika: The distinctive green, circular building housing the music production company of Devo co-founder Mark Mothersbaugh.
west-hollywood.Nancy Thompson's House \u2014 A Nightmar: Located in Hollywood's Spaulding Square neighborhood, this green-roofed Colonial-style house stood in for 1428 Elm Street in Springwood, Ohio, where Freddy Krueger famously stalked Nancy in her dreams in Wes Craven's A Nightmare on Elm Street (1984).
west-hollywood.Nancys House (A Nightmare on Elm Street): The iconic residential exterior used as the primary filming location for the 1984 horror classic.
west-hollywood.Norm's Restaurant (Googie Architecture): An iconic example of mid-century Googie design, famously captured in Ed Ruscha's 1964 painting Norm's, La Cienega, on Fire.
west-hollywood.Oki Dog: A punk-rock culinary institution known for its eccentric hot dogs and colorful clientele.
west-hollywood.Rainbow District (Near The Abbey): Near the world-famous The Abbey Food & Bar, arguably the most iconic LGBTQ+ bar in the world.
west-hollywood.Rainbow District Commercial Core: This area forms the high-density nightlife and retail core of the Rainbow District.
west-hollywood.Ramada Plaza by Wyndham (Commercial Anch: This is the site of the Ramada Plaza by Wyndham. It is architecturally significant as a large-scale commercial anchor that integrates retail (including an Enterprise Rent-A-Car and Kitchen24) into a hotel complex. It is directly across from the legendary Hugo’s Restaurant (8401 Santa Monica Blvd), a pioneer of the "health-conscious" Los Angeles dining movement since 1980.
west-hollywood.Romanesque Villa (Marilyn Monroe Residen: A high-style historic apartment complex that served as the home of Marilyn Monroe and Marlene Dietrich.
west-hollywood.Russian West Hollywood Commercial Distri: Sites adjacent to Irving’s Deli (8222) and located in the "Russian West Hollywood" district, an area characterized by a high concentration of Russian-language signage and traditional Eastern European delis.
west-hollywood.Schwab's Pharmacy (Former Site): Once the most famous drugstore in America, it was a legendary hangout for Hollywood actors and industry figures.
west-hollywood.Scream 3 (The Harper House): Featured as a location in the third installment of the Scream franchise.
west-hollywood.Sierra Towers: Designed by Jack A. Charney—a protégé of legendary modernist architects Richard Neutra and Rudolph Schindler—and completed in 1965, this 31-story building is a towering landmark of mid-century design. Situated at the base of the Hollywood Hills, it holds the distinction of being the tallest residential tower in the greater Los Angeles area, offering unparalleled prestige and privacy. Characterized by sleek, minimalist architecture with floor-to-ceiling glass doors and expansive private terraces. Provides sweeping, unobstructed jetliner views that stretch from the downtown skyline all the way to the Pacific Ocean. Maintains a long-standing reputation as an ultra-glamorous haven for the entertainment industry's elite, with past and present residents including Cher, Elton John, and Sidney Poitier.
west-hollywood.Sunset Tower Hotel: A premier example of Art Deco architecture designed by Leland A. Bryant in 1929. A legendary residence for stars like Frank Sinatra and Elizabeth Taylor.
west-hollywood.The Andalusia: A premier example of Spanish Colonial Revival architecture, these condos are a designated historic landmark.
west-hollywood.The Andaz West Hollywood (The Riot House: The Andaz West Hollywood (formerly the Continental Hyatt House). Known famously as "The Riot House," this 1963 mid-century tower was the preferred hotel for Led Zeppelin, The Who, and the Rolling Stones. It was the first hotel on the Sunset Strip to cater specifically to rock stars and featured the first rooftop pool in the city.
west-hollywood.The Black Dahlia 'Ghost' Site (Aster Mot: While Elizabeth Short's body was found in Leimert Park, she was last seen alive at various spots in Hollywood. This address was once the site of the Aster Motel, where theorists believe the actual murder may have taken place. The original motel is gone, but the block remains a key stop for those mapping the city's most enduring unsolved mystery.
west-hollywood.The Comedy Store: While the club opened in 1972, the building was formerly Ciro's, a premier nightclub of the Golden Age of Hollywood.
west-hollywood.The Doors Workshop: The former office and recording studio for the band The Doors.
west-hollywood.The Emery Residences (Near Garden of All: This is the site of the Emery Residences, but more importantly, it sits adjacent to the Garden of Allah's original footprint. While the legendary hotel was demolished in 1959, this residential pocket remains one of the most sought-after "Old Hollywood" architectural clusters in the city.
west-hollywood.The Gateway Retail Anchor: The Gateway, a major modern retail anchor that marked a significant shift in the neighborhood's urban development in the early 2000s.
west-hollywood.The Golden Girls House: While the show was set in Miami, this house was used for the exterior shots of the fictional residence.
west-hollywood.The Lot (Formerly United Artists Studio): Founded by Mary Pickford and Charlie Chaplin, this historic studio lot is the ancestral heart of independent film production in West Hollywood.
west-hollywood.The Pacific Design Center (The Blue Whal: The Pacific Design Center. Known as the "Blue Whale," this massive architectural complex designed by César Pelli is a masterwork of late-modernist design and the epicenter of the West Coast interior design industry.
west-hollywood.The Patio del Moro Apartments: Built in 1925 by Arthur and Nina Zwebell, this complex is a stunning example of the courtyard-style Spanish Colonial Revival and Andalusian architecture that defined early Hollywood luxury. Located in the Harper Avenue Historic District, it is a registered Historic Landmark that perfectly captures the romance and atmosphere of the city's golden age. Features a distinctive copper-domed Tunisian pigeon tower and a brick horseshoe-arched entrance tunnel. The lush inner courtyard boasts a grand baroque outdoor fireplace and a beautifully tiled reflecting pool. Steeped in Hollywood lore, the building was famously home to stars like Charlie Chaplin, Humphrey Bogart, and Joan Fontaine.
west-hollywood.The Pavilions (Neighborhood Landmark): The Pavilions, a primary neighborhood landmark known for its massive scale and for being a central hub of local life.
west-hollywood.The Schindler House (Birthplace of SoCal: The Schindler House. Designed by Rudolph Schindler in 1922, this is the "birthplace" of Southern California Modernism. It was designed as a cooperative live-work space for two couples—a radical architectural and social experiment for its time.
west-hollywood.The Source Restaurant: The home of the "Source Family" vegetarian restaurant, a staple of 1970s counter-culture.
west-hollywood.The Sunset Strip: The stretch of Sunset Boulevard (including locations like Rodney Bingenheimer's English Disco at 7561 Sunset Blvd) famous for its music venues, billboards, and nightlife history.
west-hollywood.The West Hollywood Library: Home to The West Hollywood Library, a 2011 LEED-certified architectural landmark designed by Johnson Favaro. The building features massive exterior murals by world-renowned street artists Shepard Fairey, Retna, and Kenny Scharf.
west-hollywood.Villa Sevilla: A Mediterranean-style "stage-set" apartment building within the Harper Avenue Historic District.
west-hollywood.Wallace Beery Complex: A Streamline Moderne complex designed by William Kesling.
west-hollywood.William Haines Residence: The former home of William Haines, known as "Hollywood's First Openly Gay Star" and a prominent interior designer. The property was also inhabited by actors John Garfield and Tallulah Bankhead.
west-hollywood.World's First LGBT Synagogue (Beth Chayi: Located in the region, Beth Chayim Chadashim is the world's first LGBT Synagogue.

westchester.Dinah's Restaurant: Classic family restaurant on S Sepulveda Blvd, a Westchester institution since 1959.
westchester.Hangar No. 1 (LAX): One of the original hangars of Los Angeles Municipal Airport (now LAX), representing the earliest phase of commercial aviation in Los Angeles. As aviation transformed the 20th century, hangars like this were at the frontier of that transformation.
westchester.Lynette Squeaky Fromme Childhood Home: The childhood residence of Manson Family member Lynette Fromme, located in a quiet suburban pocket of Westchester.

westchester.Sepulveda Rose: Local establishment on W Manchester Ave.

westchester.The Theme Building LAX (HCM #570): Space-age centerpiece of LAX; designed by Pereira & Luckman, Paul R. Williams, and Welton Becket.

westlake-hcm.Langer\u2019s Deli: Established in 1947, Langer’s sits across from MacArthur Park and remains a temple of Jewish deli culture. The brown-and-orange vinyl booths and the original Formica counters take you straight back to the 1940s.
westlake-hcm.Quality Cafe: A legendary, now-closed classic diner used in countless films and shows including Seinfeld (as Monks interior), Mad Men, and Catch Me If You Can.

westlake-macarthur-park.\"El Ultimo Beso\" Mural: Located on the side of Langer’s parking lot. A massive, cinematic mural of a couple's final kiss, capturing the romanticism and transience of the neighborhood.
westlake-macarthur-park.\"La Vida Loca\" Mural: A controversial mural sponsored by the 18th Street Gang. An essential entry for documenting how organized subcultures claim physical territory through graphic design.
westlake-macarthur-park.American Cement Building: A Brutalist masterpiece. The defining feature is the X-shaped concrete screen designed by Malcolm Leland in 1964. Built to showcase the versatility of cement as a material, capturing the transition of Wilshire Blvd from 'residential grand' to 'corporate modern.'
westlake-macarthur-park.Amok Books / Unusual Books (Closed): A legendary cult bookstore run by Stuart Swezey. It was the epicenter for 'transgressive' literature and 'Bizarro L.A.' culture in the 90s.
westlake-macarthur-park.Angelica Lutheran Church: A historic center for the sanctuary movement in the 1980s.
westlake-macarthur-park.Belmont High School: Historically the primary high school for the Westlake/Crown Hill area.
westlake-macarthur-park.CARECEN (Central American Resource Cente: This organization moved to 2845 W 7th St, but its spiritual and physical roots are firmly in Westlake/Koreatown (originally 674 S Oxford Ave).
westlake-macarthur-park.Chouinard Art Institute: The founding site of what became CalArts. Walt Disney famously supported the school here. It’s the spiritual birthplace of L.A.’s animation and conceptual art dominance.
westlake-macarthur-park.El Rescate: One of the first agencies to provide legal aid to Central American refugees in the 1980s.
westlake-macarthur-park.Immaculate Conception Church: A massive Gothic Revival landmark serving the local Catholic community.
westlake-macarthur-park.Jaime Escalante & Edward James Olmos Mur: A tribute to the Stand and Deliver legacy beside the Westlake Theatre, celebrating East L.A. excellence in the heart of Westlake.
westlake-macarthur-park.Kike\u2019s Tacos: Pronounced "Kee-Kay" (a common Spanish nickname for Enrique). It’s a staple for the local Crown Hill/Temple-Beaudry area.
westlake-macarthur-park.Langer\u2019s Deli: An L.A. institution since 1947, Langer's is widely regarded as serving the best pastrami sandwich in the country. Located across from MacArthur Park, it has survived and thrived through the neighborhood's many transformations and remains a beloved anchor of Westlake.
westlake-macarthur-park.Lewis House: A rare Queen Anne Victorian (HCM #39) designed by Joseph Cather Newsom in 1890. It’s a survivor of the era when Westlake was the 'Champs-Élysées' of Los Angeles.
westlake-macarthur-park.MacArthur Park: The namesake 32-acre park that sits at the center of the neighborhood.
westlake-macarthur-park.MacArthur Park & The MacArthur (Park Pla: MacArthur Park (formerly Westlake Park) is a historic urban green space featuring a scenic lake and the Levitt Pavilion, which hosts free summer concerts. Adjacent is The MacArthur, formerly the Park Plaza Hotel, a stunning 1925 Neo-Gothic building renowned for its grand ballroom and extensive history as a cinematic backdrop.
westlake-macarthur-park.MacArthur Park Recreation Center: A central Westlake facility providing essential community services and recreational access at the edge of the iconic park.
westlake-macarthur-park.McManus & Morgan: Historic fine paper store operating since 1923.
westlake-macarthur-park.Oscar Romero Clinic: Named after the martyred Archbishop, specifically serving the Westlake community.
westlake-macarthur-park.Pacific Dining Car (Closed): A 1921 railway car turned high-end steakhouse. It was the site of countless backroom political deals and Noir-era dinners.
westlake-macarthur-park.Park Plaza Hotel / Elks Lodge No. 99: Designed by Curlett & Beelman in 1925. It’s a Neo-Gothic/Art Deco hybrid that feels like a cathedral for high society. The interior is famously opulent, used in countless films (like Barton Fink and Drive).
westlake-macarthur-park.Rampart Village:
The History: Originally considered the "west end" outskirts of the city in the late 19th century, this area was heavily shaped by early real estate magnates like George Shatto and his wife Clarissa (who donated the land for nearby Lafayette Park). In the late 1800s, it was a major transfer point for early transit systems like the Temple Street Cable Railway. While it began as an affluent suburban enclave of large Victorian mansions, the 1920s population boom transformed the area drastically, with developers building massive brick apartment houses and converting existing mansions into multi-family housing.

Neighborhood Character: Today, Rampart Village sits right at the geographic center of the city, wedged tightly between Koreatown, Silver Lake, Echo Park, and Downtown LA. It is one of the most densely populated, highly walkable, and diverse working-class neighborhoods in Los Angeles. The architecture offers a fascinating, layered timeline of the city's growth, where surviving turn-of-the-century single-family homes (Craftsman bungalows and Queen Annes) sit shoulder-to-shoulder with 1920s Beaux-Arts apartment blocks, mid-century dingbats, and modern infill developments.
westlake-macarthur-park.Royal Viking Motel: Where Hal LeSueur, the estranged brother of movie star Joan Crawford, died in obscurity in 1963. A classic 'Hollywood Fall' story set in a mid-century motel.
westlake-macarthur-park.Selena Mural (Hector Ponce): An iconic neighborhood landmark by Hector Ponce, the master of Westlake's 'celebrity-saint' portraiture.
westlake-macarthur-park.Silver Platter: One of Los Angeles' oldest operating gay bars and a haven for the Latinx LGBTQ community.
westlake-macarthur-park.St. Thomas the Apostle Church: Located on the western edge of the district.
westlake-macarthur-park.The \"V\" Apartment: Used as a primary filming location for the 1983 sci-fi miniseries V. It represents the neighborhood's frequent use as a stand-in for 'anywhere USA' or 'dystopian urban sprawl.'
westlake-macarthur-park.The Hite Building / Aardvark Letterpress: A Morgan, Walls & Clements design featuring intricate Spanish/Churrigueresque ornamentation. Home to Aardvark Letterpress, a sanctuary for high-end typography and printing in the heart of the district.
westlake-macarthur-park.The Last Oil Well: A literal oil well hidden in the backyard of a commercial property. A reminder that L.A. was built on 'black gold.'
westlake-macarthur-park.Tiny Street: Quite literally, a street named 'Little Street' that is arguably the smallest in the district.
westlake-macarthur-park.Tommy\u2019s Original: The original 24-hour burger shack. It’s a masterpiece of 'car culture' architecture—minimal, functional, and iconic.

westwood.10919 Strathmore Dr., Westwood, Los Ange: The Strathmore Apartments, a 1937 modernist masterpiece designed by architect Richard Neutra.
westwood.10920 Wilshire Blvd (Richard Meier Tower: A 1980s office tower designed by the legendary architect Richard Meier.
westwood.Fox Bruin & Fox Village Theaters: Located just across the street from one another, these two theaters are crown jewels of 1930s Fox Studio architecture. The Fox Village Theater features an iconic 170-foot Spanish Deco tower crowned with a massive, glowing neon FOX sign that dominates the Westwood skyline. Both theaters were heavily featured in Quentin Tarantino's Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood (2019), where Margot Robbie (playing Sharon Tate) stops to watch herself in The Wrecking Crew.
westwood.Fox Village Theatre: A stunning 1931 Spanish Mission-style theater with an iconic 170-foot white tower. Featured heavily in Quentin Tarantino's Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.
westwood.Kelly Music / Tanino Ristorante: Historic Paul R. Williams-designed commercial building.
westwood.Richard Meier Tower: A 1980s office tower designed by the legendary architect Richard Meier.
westwood.Sinai Temple: The Sina Temple, a major religious landmark on the Wilshire Corridor featuring striking 1960s modernist architecture.
westwood.Stan's Donuts: Stan’s Donuts, a historic corner fixture in Westwood Village known for its distinctive signage since 1963.
westwood.Strathmore Apartments: A highly influential modernist multi-family complex designed by Richard Neutra in 1937, stepping up a steep hillside.
westwood.The Wilshire Terrace: The Wilshire Terrace, a high-end 1958 residential tower that was one of the first luxury high-rises on the "Wilshire Corridor."

whittier.2800 Workman Mill Rd, Whittier, CA: ‎ - Puente Hills Landfill  - The Puente Hills Landfill is the largest active landfill in the United States. Its buried and rotting garbage produces methane gas, which is harvested by large pipes. Noah Webb photo.
whittier.6543 Washington Ave, Whittier, CA: The History: Located in the heart of the Uptown Whittier historic district, this commercial site is part of the city's traditional pedestrian-friendly business core. The area is defined by its early 20th-century storefronts and preservation efforts that have maintained a classic "Main Street" atmosphere.
whittier.7011 Greenleaf Ave Whittier, CA 90602: Golden Triangle - Thai, Burmese & Laos Cuisine 5.
whittier.7038 Greenleaf Ave, Whittier, CA: The History: Greenleaf Avenue has served as Whittier’s primary commercial artery since the late 1800s. This specific address, currently housing Brighton Management, is part of the historic fabric that connects the city’s Quaker founding to its modern role as a regional shopping and dining destination.
whittier.Arturo's Puffy Taco: Arturo’s Puffy Taco. A culinary landmark since the 1970s, famous for its "Puffy Taco" (a San Antonio specialty). The building represents the "vernacular" roadside architecture of Whittier’s 1960s suburban expansion.
whittier.Googie Architecture Corridor (Whittier B: Googie Architecture Corridor. Whittier Boulevard is world-famous for its high concentration of Googie Architecture. Look for the cantilevered roofs and massive neon signs that define the post-war roadside aesthetic of the East Valley.
whittier.Jonathan Bailey House: The oldest residence in the city (c. 1887); a pristine example of the simple, functional architecture favored by Whittier's Quaker founders.
whittier.P\u00edo Pico State Historic Park ('El R: The restored 19th-century home of the last governor of Mexican California; a unique hybrid of traditional adobe and American-style additions.
whittier.Rose Hills & Rainbow Chapel: Rose Hills & Rainbow Chapel. One of the largest cemeteries in the world, this 1,400-acre site is an architectural museum. The Rainbow Chapel (1940s) is a stunning Mission-style landmark overlooking the San Gabriel Valley.
whittier.Steak Corral: Steak Corral. A quintessential example of "Theme" architecture from the early 1960s, featuring a wild-west exterior and wagon-wheel fixtures.
whittier.The Nixon Escape (Uptown Whittier): The Nixon Escape. Located in the Uptown Whittier Historic District, this building sits near where Richard Nixon opened his first law office. The district is characterized by late 19th-century commercial brickwork and early 20th-century Revivals.
whittier.Whittier College (Historic Core): Features several early 20th-century buildings that anchor the city's academic and Quaker identity.

wilmington.1014 Wilmington Ave., Wilmington, CA 907: South Bay Liquor & Mart‎ - Crash filming location.
wilmington.105 EI St Wilmington, CA 90744: 105 E I St Wilmington, CA 90744 - The Don Senior Apartments Signage.
wilmington.505 NAvalon Blvd, Wilmington, CA: The History: Located on the main thoroughfare of Wilmington, this facility serves as a vital community hub. It reflects the civic history of the area, providing essential services and a gathering space for the resident population near the Port of Los Angeles.
wilmington.Banning Museum: The stately 1864 Greek Revival residence of General Phineas Banning, the "Father of the Port of Los Angeles." Surrounded by towering eucalyptus trees, the 23-room mansion offers an elegant, slightly incongruous window into the Civil War era amidst the industrial sprawl of Wilmington.
wilmington.Banning Park HPOZ: A preserved district centered around the 1864 Greek Revival mansion of Phineas Banning.
wilmington.Drum Barracks: The last remaining structure of Camp Drum (later Drum Barracks), a major Union Army outpost established in 1862 to keep California in the Union during the Civil War and to mount expeditions into the Southwest. The preserved officers' quarters now serve as a museum of Civil War-era California.
wilmington.Drum Barracks Civil War Museum: The last remaining portion of Camp Drum, the Union Army headquarters in the Southwest during the Civil War.
wilmington.General Phineas Banning Residence: The 1864 Greek Revival home of General Phineas Banning, the 'Father of Los Angeles Harbor.' Banning was the transportation entrepreneur who connected Los Angeles to the sea and to the national railroad network, transforming the city's economic possibilities. The estate is now a city museum.
wilmington.Hardcore Longshore: Hardcore Longshore. This site is the heart of the "Port Identity," featuring graphics and apparel that define the local labor and industrial culture of the Port of Los Angeles.
wilmington.Los Angeles Harbor College: A sprawling community college serving the Harbor region, established in 1949.
wilmington.Rick's In & Out: Rick’s In & Out. A classic harbor-district burger stand that has served the longshoremen and port workers of Wilmington for decades.
wilmington.The Chowder Barge: The Chowder Barge, located at 611 N Henry Ford Ave in Wilmington, is one of Southern California's most distinct landmarks—a quirky, nautical-themed diner situated on a converted 1934 wooden barge. History and Atmosphere: The barge has a storied past, originally built as a support vessel for the 1935 film Mutiny on the Bounty (starring Clark Gable), where it was used to transport cameras and equipment. Before becoming a permanent restaurant fixture in the Leeward Bay Marina in 1988, it served as a Catalina Island shuttle, a houseboat, and allegedly even a brothel.
wilmington.The Powder Magazine: A small stone structure once used to store gunpowder for the Union Army, representing the strategic military importance of Wilmington in the 1860s.

wilshire-center.Wilshire Boulevard Temple: The oldest Jewish synagogue in Los Angeles; its massive Byzantine dome has been a city landmark since 1929.
wilshire-center.Wilshire Christian Church Building: A 1920s Cathedral-style church that reflects the early spiritual development of the Wilshire Center area.

wilshire-park.Wilshire Park HPOZ:
The Context: Designated as a Historic Preservation Overlay Zone (HPOZ) in 2008, Wilshire Park is a remarkably intact residential enclave.

Neighborhood Character: The area is celebrated for its diverse and well-preserved collection of early 20th-century architecture, including Craftsman, Dutch Colonial, Spanish Colonial Revival, and Prairie Style homes. It offers a quieter, residential look at the expansion of Los Angeles during the "Wilshire boom" of the 1920s.

windsor-square.Higgins-Verbeck-Hirsch Mansion: A massive Queen Anne/Craftsman hybrid mansion relocated from Wilshire Blvd to Windsor Square in 1924.
windsor-square.Historic Windsor Square Estate: Built in 1927 and designed by Robert D. Jones in the Georgian Revival style, this historic estate sits on over a third of an acre. Beyond its grand architectural pedigree, the home boasts a fun piece of 1980s pop culture history: it was featured as Matthew Broderick's house in the 1983 film WarGames. Situated behind mature magnolia trees, the home features wood siding, white brickwork, preserved original wood windows, and a detached guest house. It recently underwent a meticulous restoration that preserved its 1920s grandeur while modernizing the underlying systems.
windsor-square.Los Altos Apartments: Built in 1926 and designed by Edward B. Rust (with interior contributions reportedly by Julia Morgan), this five-story Spanish Colonial Revival building was hailed by the LA Times as a new standard of luxury. It was the first co-op on the West Coast, serving as a playground for golden-era Hollywood royalty like Bette Davis, Mae West, Douglas Fairbanks, and William Randolph Hearst with his mistress Marion Davies (whose lavish suite was reportedly designed by Julia Morgan). Instantly recognizable by its classic neon rooftop sign, the building features preserved original hardwood floors, intricate moldings, and a grand lobby. It is Los Angeles Historic-Cultural Monument #311.
windsor-square.The Snow White House (Adriana Caselotti: Nestled among the historic, meticulously planned streets of Windsor Square and the Larchmont Village shopping district, this uniquely crafted cedar home was designed and built by Adriana Caselotti — the original voice of Disney's Snow White. Fittingly, the home's architecture is deeply organic, and the front garden features a wooden bridge and a wishing well.

winnetka.Cupid's Hot Dogs: Cupid’s Hot Dogs. A legendary Valley institution since 1946. Known for its classic red-and-yellow signage, it remains a primary cultural anchor for the neighborhood.
winnetka.Winnetka Bowl & Lane 33 Bar: Winnetka Bowl. A massive 32-lane facility built in 1958. It is one of the last grand bowling palaces left in the Valley, retaining its original mid-century scale and the iconic Lane 33 Bar & Grill.
winnetka.Winnetka Convention Center: A large-scale private event venue frequently used for community-wide forums and neighborhood celebrations.

los-angeles-woodland-hills.Al Struckus House (Bruce Goff): A 1973 residential design by visionary architect Bruce Goff, featuring his signature organic and unconventional forms. One of only a handful of Goff-designed homes in Southern California.
los-angeles-woodland-hills.Bobby's Coffee Shop: Bobby’s Coffee Shop. A cherished "local’s only" diner that represents the 1960s breakfast culture of Ventura Boulevard.
los-angeles-woodland-hills.Candy Cane Lane: Candy Cane Lane. A world-famous seasonal landmark since 1952. This 8-block area represents a significant "folk art" tradition where homeowners create elaborate, massive light displays.
los-angeles-woodland-hills.Dirk Diggler's House (Boogie Nights): Located in the San Fernando Valley, this sprawling residential property is where Dirk buys his own lavish home after finding massive success in the 1970s in Paul Thomas Anderson's Boogie Nights (1997).
los-angeles-woodland-hills.Fire Station 84: A modern emergency station serving the Warner Center business district and surrounding Woodland Hills residential areas.
los-angeles-woodland-hills.Rib Ranch BBQ: Rib Ranch BBQ. A long-standing hidden gem at the base of the Topanga Canyon pass. Its rustic, Western aesthetic signals the transition from the city into the Santa Monica Mountains.
